# Adversarial review — D3 Play/Emergence lens ("THE WORLD GETS BIG")

Verified at /home/john/jumpgate, HEAD db08b51 (docs-only commit atop the briefed 88a5d85; 88a5d85 confirmed ancestor). Every load-bearing code claim in D3 was re-read from source; the verification scorecard is at the end.

## CRITICAL 1 — D2(a) "drift forever, costs zero mechanism" is FALSE: the stranded craft becomes a zombie Hauler and re-locks escrow forever (the d092496 bug class reopened through the front door)

The post-stranding sequence the design never traces:

1. `settle_contract_failure` releases the dry craft to `role = Idle`, `contract = None` (`crates/jumpgate-core/src/economy.rs:1303-1306`).
2. The ASSIGN loop claims ANY Idle scripted craft on its stagger slot — its eligibility checks are role, scripted, stagger, capacity ONLY; **no fuel read anywhere in the loop** (`economy.rs:533-560`, full loop read to ~645).
3. `resolve_contracts` escrows at accept; `try_load` finds the craft not at the pickup and dispatches the deadhead Seek with `dv = tsiolkovsky_dv(..., ships.fuel_mass[crow])` ≈ **0** (`economy.rs:796-811`).
4. The autopilot coasts permanently at `dv_remaining <= 0.0` (`autopilot.rs:61`). Fuel never re-crosses FUEL_EMPTY_EPS (the edge needs `prev > eps` strictly, `events.rs:49-51`), so `resolve_failures` — which fires only off THIS tick's FuelEmpty events — never touches it.

Result: one contract per stranded craft sits `Accepted` forever holding escrow (the Σ identity holds, but the liquidity is dead — exactly the escrow-lock class the rung's own motivation cites d092496 for fixing). Worse for THIS lens: the craft ends the run as `role == Hauler`, so the design's ADRIFT detector (`role == Idle ∧ fuel ≤ eps`, §2.2) reads FALSE — **adrift_end = 0 while a zombie drifts, the tombstone never prints, and S4's signature watchable is invisible by its own definition**. D2(a) requires a fuel-dead dispatch guard (or refuel-or-fail rule) as a named mechanism; it is not free.

## CRITICAL 2 — §5's tank derivation is arithmetically infeasible: "2.5–3× round-trip spend" and "far above FUEL_EMPTY_EPS" cannot both hold at the current craft-spec scale

Run the numbers the design declined to run. Full-throttle burn = `max_thrust/v_e·dt` = 1e-12/20·0.25 = **1.25e-14/tick** (`ship.rs:48`, spec constants `scenario.rs:88-95`). Worst common leg ~710 ticks at duty cycle < 1 (G1 #11) ⇒ round-trip spend ≤ ~1.8e-11. Tank at 2.5–3× ⇒ **~2–5e-11 — which is 20–50× BELOW `FUEL_EMPTY_EPS = 1e-9`** (`events.rs:16`). A craft spawned with that tank starts inside the edge's dead zone (predicate needs `prev > 1e-9`, `events.rs:50`; the burn predicate is `fuel_mass <= 0.0`, `ship.rs:35`, so it happily thrusts there): FuelEmpty is **structurally unfireable again**, identically to today's bake (tank == eps exactly, `scenario.rs:93,113,126`). Conversely a tank "far above eps" (≥1e-8) at current burn = ≥800k full-throttle ticks: a leg consumes <0.1% — the gauge is scenery, permille fields read 999→999, S3 dies. There is NO tank-only value satisfying §5. The fix space is a coordinated craft-spec re-bake — dry_mass + max_thrust scaled together (preserving accel, trips, brakability) so the tank lands above eps with few-trip endurance — and/or reconsidering the eps const itself. §5 hands "the tank number" to other lenses as a single knob and cites G2 #6 without computing the consequence; as written, the rung's fuel watchables (S3 gauge, S4 stranding, the FUEL line distributions) all fail at once.

## CRITICAL 3 — Self-averaging rebuild in I1: dispatch is position-blind, so "rim-workers" do not exist as a mechanism, and the pre-registered corr(radius, lag) signature rests on an unbuilt locality assumption

The ASSIGN loop reads **no craft position whatsoever**: score = `reward × (1000 − evidence penalty)` over the global Offered pool, claim by stagger slot and dense row (`economy.rs:533-645`; verified line-by-line — the only per-craft inputs are role, scripted flag, stagger phase, capacity, and the gossip/ring evidence count). A hauler at the rim takes an inner-system contract whenever score says; over the ~tens of accepts per craft per 50k run, each craft's "realized workplace radius" (median dock radius) converges on the same global route distribution — the LLN homogenization that killed the contention game, rebuilt at the craft-identity layer. S2's pre-registered "corr(radius, lag) strongly positive (the rim hears late, mechanically)" has no producing mechanism; the likely big-map reading is noise, and the design would then misread "asymmetry doesn't price into outcomes" when the true cause is "craft have no persistent workplace for asymmetry to act on." The confound MUST be pre-registered (or a locality mechanism — distance-priced dispatch via deadhead fuel cost is the world-mechanic-shaped answer once refuel exists, but the chooser currently never sees distance). Compounding it: the claim that the 6-station control correlation is "already measured ≈ 0" is a **misattribution** — the value-free finding was arm-level median credits (gossip vs ring, Δ −0.6M), never a per-craft radius/lag/credits correlation. That control has not been measured.

## CRITICAL 4 — Pirate propellant is unaddressed: a watchable-sized tank applied fleet-wide strands the predator population and kills the trophic game

Pirates fly the same spec and tank (`scenario.rs:118-130`, fuel_mass 1.0e-9 at :126); every relocation/lurk/hideout Seek draws a fuel-derived Δv budget (G1 #6, pirate.rs:481-486 per grounding; the hideout commute alone is repeated 1.4 AU-class legs). The refuel verb D3 assumes is dock+wallet+price-shaped for haulers; the pirate purchase arm buys escorts at the hideout only (`economy.rs:1041-1067`) and pirates are information-blind (OD-6) — a pirate fuel-price read is a new tension, not a freebie. If the tank re-bake (CRITICAL 2's coordinated version) lands fleet-wide, pirates run dry within a few relocations → predator collapse → the ecosystem oscillation (the one judged success) dies on the big map. The design sizes the tank purely for hauler narrative and never mentions pirates. Required surface: either per-class tank asymmetry (an explicit owner-visible decision — an effectively-infinite unpriced pirate tank is a design statement, adjacent to a dead knob) or a pirate refuel path (scope, OD-6 tension). Silence is not an option.

## Major (non-critical) findings

- **M1 — ContractFailed payload lies on the degrade path.** The stale-corp-row arm skips the refund leg but still fails the contract (`economy.rs:1290-1295`); `escrow_refunded_micros` must report the actual (0) transfer there or the event misstates the ledger. The design doesn't handle the arm.
- **M2 — `tank_before_permille` is NOT "derived from the already-decided integer purchase."** It requires `fuel_mass` (f64 state) ÷ effective `fuel_capacity` — a fresh f64 division whose rounding rule the design must pin (floor, like the scenario's top-53-bit precedent). Deterministic, but the "no raw f64 / agree by construction" claim and Test 2 as written are wrong for the *before* field.
- **M3 — S1's news deserts silently depend on the unwritten frontier factory's contract topology.** Gossip rides hauler dockings; dockings follow contract wiring. Today's wiring is symmetric per-tier (sources 0-2 → dests 3-5, `scenario.rs:150-207`); a frontier factory that reproduces symmetric all-tier wiring equalizes traffic and the radius-correlated deserts may simply not form. Pre-register the dependency and point it at the scenario lens (hub-and-spoke vs all-pairs is the actual lever).
- **M4 — D4 overreaches: the META line cannot delete media_log.py's mirrors.** META carries structure (counts, radii); media_log.py's trap is MediaCfg *knob value* mirrors as CLI defaults (G4 #20). Those survive unless knob values are also emitted (or the script reads the runner's `sets=` echo). "python mirrors deleted" holds for sweep_trophic's N_HAULERS (verified hardcoded at `sweep_trophic.py:72`) only.
- **M5 — `adrift_end` sourced from "the epilogue count" couples a machine line to the chronicle printer.** Compute from final world state directly. The detector itself also misses dry pirates (role Pirate, never Idle) and — per CRITICAL 1 — the actual zombie end-state (role Hauler). The honest predicate is fuel-based, not role-based.
- **M6 — FUEL line needs a zero-refuel sentinel.** `median/min_tank_permille` are undefined at refuels=0 (guaranteed on 6-station band runs if the line is emitted there); MEDIA's 0-sentinel precedent (`trophic_run.rs:404-417` region, verified) must be copied or the regex/lockstep aborts the sweep.

## Minor

- ContractFailed-for-FuelEmpty-only means robbery-failed contracts emit no ContractFailed — any panel counting failures by event undercounts; document the asymmetry where the panel reads.
- I1's "workplace radius" is underspecified (from-radius, to-radius, or both ends of the accept route?); I2's `strandings_near` has no definition of "near."
- A broke craft dry AT a dock prints "ADRIFT" while parked at a station — wording, not logic.
- Citation drift of a few lines throughout (e.g. chronicle_subject actually ~236-262, print_chronicle ~264-303) — all materially correct.
- Event stream is an unbounded Vec (`events.rs:19-31`) — full retention confirmed, so the epilogue/gossip-log joins are sound; note ThrustApplied volume grows with map size (existing condition, not new).

## Frame audit (PDR-0006): clean, with the findings above as the only caveats

Windows language is consistent; the stranding-frequency expectation is correctly framed as a window with a named deferred-ladder trigger; no shaping (the 50% refuel threshold is a world-level config scalar in the buy_headroom mold, `economy.rs:1091-1097` verified, not a per-craft taste); micros-in-events does not cross the valuations law — `Robbed { value_micros }` and the unhashed-event-stream comment verified at `contract.rs:97-106`; D7's non-bundling statement is the right shape. The one frame-adjacent wobble is the CRITICAL 3 misattribution of an unmeasured control as "already measured."

## Verification scorecard (claims re-read from source, all confirmed unless noted)

Confirmed: no-failure-event comment + `FailureCause` enum (`economy.rs:1218-1246`); settle legs and Idle release (`economy.rs:1262-1308`); `N_HAULERS = 12` mirror (`sweep_trophic.py:70-72`); events-unhashed comment (`contract.rs:97-98`); `Robbed.value_micros` (`contract.rs:100-106`); `per_craft_credits` = all craft (`diagnostics.rs:581`); `per_station_alerts/contacts` (`diagnostics.rs:533-541`); gossip-log born/heard/accept lines with hops/rob_tick/claimed (`trophic_run.rs:186-236`); `chronicle_subject` default-skip + GossipHeard craft arm (`trophic_run.rs:236-262`); consecutive-repeat collapse + gossip filter (`trophic_run.rs:264-303`); `--chronicle-gossip-min-micros` and `--assert-no-fuel-empty` flags (`trophic_run.rs:44-68, 445-448`); RESULT/MEDIA/ASSIGN lines + sweep abort-on-missing (`trophic_run.rs:383-437`, `sweep_trophic.py:111-116`); endurance panel text (`sweep_trophic.py:151-152`); VoI PDR framing (`sweep_trophic.py:273-279`); positive control (`sweep_trophic.py:345-361`); desync-by-construction + working-capital gate (`economy.rs:993-998, 1085-1097`); FuelEmpty edge + eps (`events.rs:16, 49-51`); burn predicate `<= 0.0` and clamp (`ship.rs:35, 47`); band tank == eps (`scenario.rs:93, 113, 126`); fuel-empty fixture at 1.06e-9 (`world.rs:2196-2205`); deadhead dv from current fuel (`economy.rs:796-811`); unbounded event retention (`events.rs:19-31`). **Not confirmed:** the "6-station control corr ≈ 0 already measured" claim (misattribution, CRITICAL 3); the implied feasibility of §5's tank numbers (CRITICAL 2); the implied "Idle drifting" end-state of a stranded craft (CRITICAL 1).
criticals: D2(a) 'drift forever costs zero mechanism' is false: ASSIGN has no fuel check (economy.rs:533-560), so a stranded craft is re-claimed, escrowed, and dispatched with dv=tsiolkovsky(fuel~0)=0 (economy.rs:796-811) -> coasts forever at dv_remaining<=0 (autopilot.rs:61) with the contract Accepted and escrow locked permanently (the d092496 escrow-lock class reopened); the craft ends role==Hauler so the design's ADRIFT detector (role==Idle && fuel<=eps) reads 0 adrift while a zombie drifts -- S4's tombstone never prints. Needs a fuel-dead dispatch guard as a named mechanism.

Sect-5 tank arithmetic is infeasible: 2.5-3x round-trip spend at current burn (1.25e-14/tick, ship.rs:48; <=710-tick legs) gives a tank of ~2-5e-11, which is 20-50x BELOW FUEL_EMPTY_EPS=1e-9 (events.rs:16) -- the whole tank sits in the edge dead zone (needs prev>1e-9, events.rs:50; craft still thrusts, ship.rs:35) so FuelEmpty stays structurally unfireable; conversely a tank far above eps never visibly moves. No tank-only value satisfies both Sect-5 requirements: a coordinated dry_mass+max_thrust+capacity re-bake (accel-preserving) and/or an eps decision is required, and the design never surfaces this.

Self-averaging rebuild in I1: the ASSIGN loop is completely position-blind (economy.rs:533-645 -- score = reward x evidence only, no distance/position read), so 'rim-workers' have no producing mechanism; per-craft workplace radius converges to the global contract pool over tens of accepts (the contention-game LLN death at the craft-identity layer) and the pre-registered corr(radius,lag) 'strongly positive mechanically' is unsupported. Additionally the claimed 6-station control 'correlation ~= 0 already measured' is a misattribution -- the value-free finding was arm-level median credits, never a per-craft correlation; that control was never measured.

Pirate propellant is unaddressed: pirates fly the same spec/tank (scenario.rs:118-130) and burn fuel on every reach-bounded relocation and hideout commute, but the refuel verb is hauler/dock/wallet-shaped, the pirate purchase arm buys escorts only (economy.rs:1041-1067), and pirates are information-blind (OD-6) so a fuel-price read is a new tension. A watchable-sized tank applied fleet-wide strands the predator population within a few relocations -> trophic collapse on the big map. Either per-class tank asymmetry (an explicit owner decision; an unpriced infinite pirate tank borders on a dead-knob design statement) or a pirate refuel path must be surfaced -- the design is silent.

design.design_md:
# D4 — Trophic Forward-Compat: Pirates on the Big Map

Lens scope: keep the predator–prey game ALIVE and FELT when the map grows, without violating OD-6 (pirates information-blind, geometry-only relocation), without taste scalars, without shaping, and without building any police-rung mechanic early. Everything below is verified against HEAD 88a5d85; all paths relative to `/home/john/jumpgate/crates/jumpgate-core/src/` unless noted.

## 0. The one bug-class finding this lens surfaced: the haven-lurk leak

**VERIFIED, code-vs-doc mismatch.** The brain's doc says a pirate "seeking the hideout" maps to no current lurk and forces a fresh draw (`pirate.rs:575-577`: "None (Idle / seeking the hideout / a non-station body) -> draw a fresh lurk"). The code does NOT deliver that: `nav_lurk` resolves *any* station whose body matches the nav destination (`pirate.rs:578-583`), and in the band the hideout body 6 HOSTS a station (`scenario.rs:156`, body_index 6; haven located at `pirate.rs:540-544`). Arrival never clears nav to Idle (arrival is an edge event; `NavState::Seeking` persists and the autopilot loiters — `world.rs:1051-1058`, no Idle transition anywhere in the step path). So the normal post-refuge sequence is: lie low → seek hideout (`pirate.rs:556-573`) → arrive, nav still `Seeking{hideout}` → lie-low expires → `nav_lurk = Some(haven)` → **the pirate camps and hunts at the haven station** until the hunger-stagger relocation moves it (food resets to exactly `grubstake` at starvation, `pirate.rs:680-681`; hungry is strict `<` (`pirate.rs:608-610`) so it goes hungry after one upkeep tick, then waits up to `relocate_period` 2500 for its slot, with a `stay_milli` 500 coin to *keep* the haven per check — `pirate.rs:611-628`). Expected haven dwell ≈ 1–3k ticks per starvation cycle, robbing traffic at the station where it fences — exactly what the haven exclusion (`pirate.rs:537-539`, `world.rs:406-415`) was built to prevent, leaking in through the one path that doesn't go through `relocate_lurk_target`.

On today's map this is a bounded blemish. On the big map it scales up: every starvation cycle ends at the hideout, the new outermost station is the frontier's anchor, and (per the food-band arithmetic below) starvation cycles per run roughly double. **In-rung fix:** in the `nav_lurk` derivation, treat `nav_lurk == haven_station` as `None` (force a fresh reach-bounded draw from the hideout position — which is also exactly what produces the frontier danger gradient, §3). One-line semantic change + test (post-refuge pirate never inherits the haven as lurk). Hash consequences: no format/column change, but it changes the Piracy draw sequence on the band scenario — a behavior-change single-cause commit; any pinned band-trajectory literal re-derives via print_golden.

## 1. Reach and adjacency on the wide map

Mechanism unchanged: `relocate_lurk_target` stays geometry-only by type (`pirate.rs:447-458`), uniform-in-reach (`pirate.rs:460-464`), marooned breakout uniform over ALL huntable stations (`pirate.rs:466-476`). The function is already n-generic; map growth needs zero code change here.

What changes is the *semantics of the number*. Reach is measured against instantaneous chord distances between co-orbiting stations: adjacent rings at radii r₁<r₂ range from r₂−r₁ (conjunction) to r₁+r₂ (opposition). So "in reach" is a **blinking adjacency graph**:

- **Inner map** (0.35–1.4 AU, periods 9.6k–76.5k ticks per G1 #22): adjacency blinks within a single run — neighbor sets churn, pirates percolate.
- **Outer map** (2–3 AU, periods 130k–240k): the synodic period of the two outermost rings is ~560k ticks (computed from G1 #22 periods, T∝a^1.5), vastly longer than any run — **outer adjacency is frozen per seed**. Whether two frontier stations are mutually reachable is seed geography, set at reset, sampled only across the ensemble.

That frozen outer graph is the structural substrate of haves/have-nots: some seeds give a frontier pirate a two-station hunting circuit; others maroon it the moment it starves. The marooned breakout (`pirate.rs:466-476`) is the relief valve and becomes a **frontier signature event** — committal, unbounded, chronicle-visible.

**Knob: `pirate_max_reach_au` — keep 0.6, but SET IT EXPLICITLY in the new factory.** The band currently inherits the default silently (`config.rs:303`; absent from the `scenario.rs:216-227` band block) — on the map where it becomes the primary felt lever it must be visible in the band. Derivation discipline: print reach ÷ adjacent-Δr per ring pair in the runner's config echo; the doc-contract "1-2 neighbors, never the whole map" (`config.rs:264-266`) is then checkable per map rather than folklore. At the reference map (n=10, 0.35–3.0 AU, near-linear spacing → Δr ≈ 0.29), 0.6 covers radial neighbors near conjunction everywhere but leaves outer pairs out of reach most of their synodic phase — locality with teeth, no value change needed. Config-identical on the old band (0.6 == default ⇒ config_hash unmoved if set explicitly there too).

## 2. Hideout: one mixing chamber, not a territory system

**Recommendation: a single hideout = the new outermost body, unchanged mechanism.** Reasons, in code:

- The hideout already functions as the map's **mixing chamber**: post-refuge re-draws (after the §0 fix) are reach-bounded *from the hideout's position* (`pirate.rs:586-598` draws from `ships.pos[row]`, which post-refuge IS the hideout), reaching outer stations when in phase, else marooned-breakout uniform over the whole map. Pirates re-enter the world from the frontier — the inner core is reachable only by breakout or chained relocation. That is the frontier-core gradient, free.
- Lying-low pirates are inert to encounters (`pirate.rs:139` skips on `lie_low_until`), so hideout commutes add no predation; they add *absence* — and the commute cost now scales with map size (~710 ticks one-way from 0.35 AU to a 3.0 AU hideout per G1 #11 trip arithmetic), eating refuge time, not hunt time (`lie_low_until` is wall-clock, `pirate.rs:680`). 4000-tick lie-low comfortably covers the worst commute + the shopping dwell.
- The haven station on the new outermost body **must stay a vendor** — the pirate escort-purchase arm settles only while hiding AT the hideout with a vendor at the dock (`economy.rs:1041-1067`: lying-low check, hideout-radius check, escort at full price; the band test pins this structurally at `scenario.rs:358-362`). Carry that assertion into the new factory's shape test.
- Per-region hideouts require `hideout_body_index: u32` (`config.rs:271-272`) to become a list plus an assignment rule — a territory primitive. Territory is meaningful when heat has geography, i.e. when police exist. Building it now is a dead structure with no consumer (violates the two-sided OD-5b instinct applied to mechanics). **Named seam, not built** (§7).

Watchable: hideout-commute fraction of pirate lifetime (derivable from the per-window lurk/lie-low snapshot, §5) — if the chronicle shows pirates spending the run commuting, the owner's lever is `starve_lie_low_ticks` or hideout choice, at the console.

## 3. Keeping boom-bust alive: pool, food band, and the saturation guard

The encounter envelope is scale-invariant (engage only in the braking cone within ~8e-3 AU of a dock; `pirate.rs:154-160`, G1 #16), so predation intensity is purely *who is lurking where prey docks*. Map growth dilutes that three ways: more stations per pirate, fewer trips per window (legs grow as √distance, G1 #11: worst leg 445→710 ticks at 3 AU), and more search time before a pirate finds a feeding lurk.

The concentrator in this system is deliberate: **selection, not perception.** Relocation is traffic-blind by type (OD-6, `pirate.rs:447-458`); what concentrates pirates onto hubs is differential starvation — a fed pirate camps (`pirate.rs:600-614`), a starved one is recycled through the hideout and re-scattered. The selection time constant is one starvation cycle: runway = grubstake/upkeep = 100_000/12 ≈ 8333 ticks, cycle ≈ 12_333 (the guard arithmetic, `scenario.rs:376-385`). A 50k run gives ~4 selection generations; that was enough at 6 stations. At 10+ stations with thinner traffic, the honest risks are NoCycle/news-desert (predation never gets traction), not Saturated.

**Scaling rules (starting values, console-walked like session 2):**

- **Pool: pirates = n_stations, haulers = 2×n_stations.** The 2-per-station spawn requires haulers ≡ 0 mod stations (`scenario.rs:107-108`); pirates all spawn at the hideout and get reset-scattered (`scenario.rs:118-130`, `world.rs:396-431`). At n=10: expected-active = 10×8333/12333 ≈ 6 ≤ 8 = stations−2 — the Saturated guard (`scenario.rs:381-385`) holds with slack; its RHS grows with n so it stops binding, and the binding risk flips to scarcity. Keep the guard assertion in the new factory's test anyway (it documents the ceiling).
- **Food band: raise `food_per_unit_micros` 10_000 → 15_000; keep upkeep 12, grubstake 100k, starve_lie_low 4000.** Derivation: per-lurk feed rate ∝ (hauler arrivals per station per window) ≈ haulers × trips/hauler/window ÷ n_stations. Reference estimate: today ~5 arrivals/station/window vs ~3.2 on the reference map (12→20 haulers, round-trips lengthen by the √-law, 6→10 stations) — a ×1.56 dilution; compensate on the meal-value side, not metabolism, so the duty-cycle arithmetic (and the guard) is untouched. Band identities still pass a fortiori: one qty-5 rob = 75k ≥ 2 windows of upkeep (48k) — now ~3.1 windows (`scenario.rs:392-396` form); grubstake > one window (24k) unchanged (`scenario.rs:397-400`); ransom 6M ≥ escort L1 5M unchanged (`scenario.rs:401-404`). The real calibration input is the printed `laden_trips_per_window` ("THE spec-§4 CALIBRATION INPUT", `examples/trophic_run.rs:361`) — re-walk against the measured value, exactly the session-2 procedure.
- **`rob_cooldown` 600: keep, accept the documented semantic drift.** Its doc says "~one trip-time" (`config.rs:258-259`); on the big map it spans one short inner leg to half a frontier leg. Changing it churns the band for no observed need; document the drift in the factory comment and watch double-rob incidents at hubs.
- **`relocate_period` 2500 / `stay_milli` 500: keep.** The pre-registered lag-vs-relocation window (§6) is anchored on 2500; moving it this rung would un-register the prediction.

## 4. The frontier-core gradient without police: is it felt?

Two distinct danger textures fall out of the verified mechanics, and they are different enough to be felt:

- **Frontier (hideout-adjacent):** high pirate FLUX — every starvation cycle re-enters here; post-refuge draws are reach-bounded from the hideout so hideout-phase-adjacent stations get first-lurk priority; but traffic is thin, so pirates starve out and the danger is *episodic* (marauders passing through). Plus the breakout departures: committal cross-map flights.
- **Core hubs:** persistent CAMPED danger — any pirate that lands on a hub feeds, and a fed pirate never relocates (`pirate.rs:608-614`). Risk history accretes on specific routes (the heterogeneity the instrument measures).

Without police, the gradient's axes are distance-from-hideout and traffic density — and crucially they are *anti-correlated* on this map (hubs are inner, hideout is outermost), so the two regimes don't blur. Honest tension, stated: this gradient is an equilibrium *prediction*, not a guarantee — if selection is too slow at scale, pirates die on the road and both textures collapse into NoCycle. That's what the windows in §6 are for, and the levers (pool, food band, reach) are all named knobs.

What I explicitly do NOT propose: any traffic-weighting, any pirate read of prices/stock/evidence, any per-craft scalar. The relocation type signature is the firewall (`pirate.rs:447-451`) and stays.

## 5. Instrumentation (designer's windows; no new stages, no hashed state)

- **`TrophicSample` gains three pure-read fields** (sampled in `diagnostics::sample_window`, `diagnostics.rs:437+`): `per_station_lurking_pirates: Vec<u32>` (active pirates whose nav body resolves to each station — the danger-geography map), `pirates_commuting: u32` (lying-low and not yet at hideout), `pirates_at_haven: u32`. Unhashed diagnostics; JSONL additive (the media-fields precedent, `diagnostics.rs:101-139`).
- **Runner-side per-window per-pirate lurk JSONL** in `examples/trophic_run.rs` (pure read of nav + positions): lurk station, food, lie-low state. From this the analysis derives lurk dwell-time distributions, breakout rate by region (a lurk jump > reach = a breakout signature), and hideout-commute fraction — no core change, no event.
- **Classifier recalibration is part of the rung** (instrument maintenance, pre-registered as *procedure*): `HHI_NORM_MIN_MILLI = 2204` and `HOT_PERSISTENCE_SLACK_CHANGES = 3` were margin-midpoint fits on 6-station labeled runs and are map-size-sensitive by their own docs (`diagnostics.rs:30-44, 46-57` — "even spread over m ≫ k routes reads ≈ 1000·k/m"). Re-run the labeled-run protocol on the new map: band baseline vs the hungry-roamer disease control (`reach=999, stay=0, upkeep=200, grubstake=2e9` — `sweep_trophic.py:32-40`), re-fit both midpoints, record old/new in the consts' doc comments. The hungry-roamer positive control MUST still read RiskEqualized on the new map (`sweep_trophic.py:352-360`) — if it doesn't, fix the instrument before tuning the world.
- **Fix the `N_HAULERS = 12` mirror in `python/analysis/sweep_trophic.py:72`** (used to slice hauler wallets at :288) — parameterize from the scenario or a runner-printed count, or the VoI panel silently corrupts on the new map.
- **`Verdict::Saturated` keeps NO classification arm** (`diagnostics.rs:152-155`). This rung moves *away* from saturation; building the arm now is speculative. Deferred means not built.
- **Ensemble default: 20 seeds × arms** (the basin-chaos lesson; 4 was far too few). The sweep's RESULT/MEDIA line-lockstep abort (`sweep_trophic.py:111-116` per G4 #17) means the new scenario's runner must emit both lines — same binary, new factory flag, no format change.

## 6. Pre-registered windows for the big map (recorded, never gated)

Baked into the lab before the first big-map run; each names its disambiguator:

1. **Saturation → Localized**: `media_classify` majority-reads Localized at default media knobs; `escaped_milli` drops out of the ≥950 band (expect 600–900); `stations_with_news` plateaus below n. Disambiguate genuine locality from "coverage merely got harder" (the clause hardens mechanically with n, `diagnostics.rs:424-429`) via the per-station news-desert map + escaped_milli jointly (G4 #24).
2. **Hearing lag exceeds the predator clock**: median `heard_lag` > 2500 (= `relocate_period`); p90 well past `evidence_window` 4000. This must come from hop-chain length (gossip rides docking haulers only), since single legs only double (G1 #11).
3. **News geography widens**: hub/backwater alert ratio past 3.0 (from 2.5–3.0 at 6 stations).
4. **The flip share becomes valuable**: with `staleness_from_rob_tick=true`, the gossip-vs-ring credit delta on clean seeds moves from −0.6M toward ≥ 0, and hauler outcomes stratify by home geography (haves/have-nots arcs visible in the chronicle). Direction registered, not magnitude.
5. **Boom-bust survives scale**: `classify` reads Alive on the majority of seeds *after* the console band-walk, with ≥3 active-pirate alternations (`CYCLE_MIN_ALTERNATIONS`, `diagnostics.rs:28`); record the NoCycle/PermanentPeace census. Expectation: failures skew NoCycle (scarcity), not Saturated.
6. **Danger geography is bimodal**: per-station robs and lurk dwell separate into persistent-hub vs episodic-frontier; breakout rate concentrates in the outer region.
7. **Run length**: my lens needs ≥3 starvation cycles (~12.3k each) AND outer-map relocation epochs per run; at 50k that's marginal once commutes lengthen the effective cycle. Recommend 100k standard big-map run (50 windows of the unchanged W=2000 — *better* per-window statistics than fewer/wider windows, and the food-band arithmetic stays anchored to W). Requires `ephemeris_window` ≥ run length (bodies silently freeze past it, `ephemeris.rs:106-111`); 150k × ~11-13 bodies ≈ 80–95 MB precompute — fine (shared decision with the map/scenario lens).

## 7. Seams the police/navy rung will need — NAMED, none built

- **Heat already exists as the wanted-level substrate**: per-pirate `notoriety` + `heat_threshold`/decay (`pirate.rs:686-700`, `config.rs:295-299`). Police consume heat; nothing to add now.
- **Bounty board = a media channel whose consumer is police** (OD-5b two-sided: no consumer yet ⇒ no channel yet). The mint site is the existing rob path; do not build.
- **Patrol pressure enters `relocate_lurk_target` as an exclude-set**, the same shape as today's haven exclusion (`exclude: Option<usize>` → future `&[usize]`), preserving the geometry-only type firewall. Type change is cut-priced and named here so nobody reaches for a traffic weight instead.
- **Strength comparison is the combat seam**: `strength(role, upgrades, trophic)` at `pirate.rs:142` — a Police role slots into the same eligibility comparison.
- **Hideout list (territory)** per §2 — lands when heat has geography.
- **Fleet ledger caveat stands**: UpgradeLevels (hulls/escorts) must not outlive the future fleet rung; this lens adds nothing to it.

## 8. Stage placement and hashing impact

- **No new stages.** Brain stays 1c2, encounters 3b2, population 3b3 (`world.rs:729-735, 946-994` ordering law untouched). Snapshot fields are sample-time pure reads.
- **No HASH_FORMAT_VERSION bump from this lens.** Reach/pool/band/hideout are config; the new factory moves config_hash by construction (cardinality folds before elements, `config.rs:570-573`); `GOLDEN_CONFIG_HASH` is pinned to the `sample()` fixture (`config.rs:745`), untouched. RouteEvidence at n=10 hashes 100 rings × 9 words (word 29) — cardinality-driven content, not a format change (G1 #18). **This rung, from D4's side, is therefore NOT the OD-2 cut-2 format bump** — the ring + risk_appetite retirement bundle stays parked unless another lens forces a bump, in which case that bundling goes to the owner explicitly (it is listed as a panel-level decision, not absorbed here).
- **The haven-lurk fix (§0) is hash-moving on trajectories** (Piracy draw sequence changes) but not on format or config: single-cause behavior commit, print_golden re-derivation for any pinned trajectory literal.
- **Pirate fuel** (§ decision D4-3): recommendation keeps pirate `BaseSpec` at the ×10 endurance while haulers get whatever tank re-bake the refuel lenses land. `CraftInit.spec` is already per-craft (`scenario.rs:109-129` clones per push) — zero new mechanism, zero new knob, pure factory config. Pirate dv budgets re-derive from current fuel at every seek (`pirate.rs:481-486`, `world.rs:421-430`), so if the owner later unifies, refuel composes cleanly with pirate nav.

## 9. Tests

1. `scenario_frontier` shape test mirroring `scenario_trophic_shape` (`scenario.rs:337-466`): n stations ascending in band; haulers ≡ 0 mod n; hideout = outermost; **haven station is a vendor** (the pirate escort settle path); saturation guard at new n; food-band identities at the new values; all-scripted; explicit reach set.
2. **Haven-lurk regression**: a pirate whose lie-low expires with nav `Seeking{hideout}` must NOT lurk the haven — next brain pass draws a fresh reach-bounded lurk (unit fixture in `pirate.rs` tests; plus the doc at `pirate.rs:575-577` becomes true).
3. `relocate_lurk_target` wide-geometry property tests (fn already n-generic): reach < min chord ⇒ always breakout; reach ≥ max chord ⇒ never; haven never returned by either branch.
4. Classifier re-fit: labeled big-map runs (baseline vs hungry-roamer control) recorded; consts updated with fit provenance in doc comments; positive control reads RiskEqualized on the new map (instrument-kill).
5. Sample-field test: `per_station_lurking_pirates` sums to active non-commuting pirates; JSONL additive (no pre-existing key moves).
6. Determinism: new scenario byte-stable across two resets + N-tick runs (existing pattern); band scenario goldens re-derived once for the §0 fix in its own commit.
7. Sweep lockstep: new runner invocation emits RESULT/MEDIA/ASSIGN lines; `sweep_trophic.py` hauler-count parameterization tested against both scenarios.

## 10. Explicitly out of this lens

Police/navy/bounties, maydays, in-flight detour, comms relay, pirate gossip reads/couriers (pirates stay blind at all three layers — `world.rs:295-302`, `media.rs:278-280`, `pirate.rs:289-290`), corruption arming beyond the existing anchor knob, gym pull, ring retirement. No dead knobs: every value named above is either an existing knob re-derived or structural factory config.

# D4 Owner Decision Points — Pirates on the Big Map

## D4-1: Hideout — single (new outermost) vs per-region havens
- **(a) Single hideout = new outermost body** (mechanism unchanged; haven station stays a vendor for the pirate escort path, `economy.rs:1041-1067`). The hideout doubles as the map's mixing chamber: every starvation cycle re-enters the world from the frontier, which IS the frontier-core gradient.
- **(b) Per-region hideouts** — `hideout_body_index` becomes a list + an assignment rule; shorter commutes, but it's a territory primitive with no consumer until police make heat geographic, and it dilutes the frontier gradient this rung wants to measure.
- **Recommendation: (a).** Per-region havens are a NAMED police-rung seam (territory). Watch hideout-commute fraction in the new lurk window; if commutes eat the game, the console lever is `starve_lie_low_ticks`, not new structure.

## D4-2: Pirate pool scaling — fixed 6 vs scale with the map
- **(a) Keep 6 pirates** on 10 stations: expected-active ≈ 4 over a map ~1.7× the station count and ~2× the leg times — predation likely starves into NoCycle.
- **(b) pirates = n_stations, haulers = 2×n_stations** (spawn rule requires the multiple, `scenario.rs:107-108`), food_per_unit 10k→15k (feed-rate dilution derivation in design §3), everything else held; band re-walked at console against the printed `laden_trips_per_window`, exactly the session-2 procedure. Saturated guard holds with slack (6 ≤ 8).
- **Recommendation: (b).** The guard stops binding as n grows; the live risk flips to scarcity, and (b) starts the console walk inside the plausible band instead of below it.

## D4-3: Pirate fuel under the tank re-bake (the cross-lens coupling)
If the refuel lenses shrink hauler endurance so propellant binds, pirates sharing that spec can strand — and a stranded pirate is a permanently lost predator (no despawn, no rescue, `stores.rs:220-224`): predation decays monotonically and the rung kills its own boom-bust.
- **(a) Pirates refuel at the haven** (paid, ransom credits, one policy line in the lying-low shop path). Honest single physics, but couples pirate viability to ransom RNG (an unlucky pirate strands broke) and adds a pirate-economy balancing problem mid-rung; also interacts with the dead PriceCfg (`scenario.rs:242`, prices all zero today).
- **(b) Pirates keep the ×10 endurance spec; only haulers get the re-bake.** `CraftInit.spec` is already per-craft (`scenario.rs:109-129`) — zero new mechanism, zero knob, pure factory config. Not a taste scalar: roles already differ structurally.
- **(c) Shared spec, stranding-as-drama.** Marooned-wanderer tragedies in the chronicle, but attrition is a one-way door to PermanentPeace-by-accident.
- **Recommendation: (b) this rung**, with a named trigger for unification: pirates join the fuel economy when the console shows ransom income comfortably covering projected fuel spend (watch via the existing ThrustApplied per-leg spend signal, G2 #20). Surfacing rather than silently deciding: if the panel prefers (a), it is the same refuel verb + one policy arm, and D4 has no structural objection — only the attrition risk above.

## D4-4: Fix the haven-lurk leak this rung?
Post-refuge pirates currently inherit the HAVEN as their lurk (code `pirate.rs:578-583` contradicts its own doc at :575-577 whenever the hideout body hosts a station — it does, `scenario.rs:156`; arrival never clears nav, `world.rs:1051-1058`) and hunt where they fence for ~1–3k ticks per starvation cycle.
- **(a) Fix now** (treat haven-resolved nav_lurk as None → fresh draw): one-line semantics + regression test; hash-moving on band trajectories (Piracy draw sequence), so it's a single-cause behavior commit with golden re-derivation — cheapest landed alongside the rung that doubles starvation-cycle frequency.
- **(b) Leave it and watch**: zero golden churn, but the leak scales with the big map and contaminates the frontier-gradient windows (haven robs read as frontier danger).
- **Recommendation: (a).** It aligns code with already-documented intent, and the big map is exactly where the leak stops being a blemish.

## D4-5: Classifier recalibration scope
`HHI_NORM_MIN_MILLI=2204` and `HOT_PERSISTENCE_SLACK_CHANGES=3` are 6-station margin-midpoint fits, map-size-sensitive by their own docs (`diagnostics.rs:30-57`).
- **(a) Re-fit this rung** via the labeled-run protocol (baseline vs hungry-roamer control) on the new map; record old/new fit provenance; instrument-kill check (control must read RiskEqualized) re-run.
- **(b) Read the new map with stale calibration** and annotate every reading.
- **Recommendation: (a).** It's instrument maintenance, pre-registered as procedure (not number); with stale thresholds the rung's central trophic readings are uninterpretable. Also fold in the `sweep_trophic.py:72` N_HAULERS hardcode fix — without it the VoI panel silently corrupts.

## D4-6: Standard big-map run length — 50k vs 100k
- **(a) 50k**: unchanged lab shape, but ~4 starvation generations shrink toward 3 once hideout commutes lengthen the cycle, and per-window event counts thin; cycle detection (≥3 alternations) gets marginal.
- **(b) 100k standard** with W=2000 unchanged (50 windows — better statistics, food-band arithmetic stays anchored) and `ephemeris_window` grown to ≥ run length (bodies silently freeze past it, `ephemeris.rs:106-111`; ~80–95 MB precompute at 150k × ~13 bodies — fine on this hardware).
- **Recommendation: (b)** for the big-map lab default (shared call with the scenario/run-length lens; D4's requirement is ≥3 full selection generations + outer-map relocation epochs per run). Note: 100k runs also re-open the door for fuel to bind even at milder tank re-bakes (the 80k-thrusting-tick endurance ceiling), which the refuel lenses may welcome.

## Panel-level flag (not D4's to decide)
This lens requires NO hash-format bump. If another lens's design forces one, the OD-2 cut-2 bundle (RouteEvidence ring retirement + `risk_appetite` ghost removal) must be surfaced to the owner as an explicit bundling decision — per standing instruction, never silently absorbed.

critique.findings_md:
# Adversarial review — D4 "Trophic Forward-Compat: Pirates on the Big Map"

Verified against the live tree (HEAD db08b51; `git diff --stat 88a5d85..HEAD -- crates/ python/` is empty — only docs/posts landed since, so the design's 88a5d85 basis is still the code that compiles). All paths under `/home/john/jumpgate/crates/jumpgate-core/src/` unless noted.

## What survives attack (re-verified, not taken on faith)

- **The §0 haven-lurk leak is REAL — D4's strongest contribution, confirmed end-to-end.** `nav_lurk` resolves any station whose body matches the nav dest (pirate.rs:578-583), directly contradicting its own doc ("seeking the hideout → None", pirate.rs:575-577). The hideout body 6 DOES host a station (scenario.rs:156, `body_index: 6`, and it's a vendor). Lying-low pirates seek the hideout (pirate.rs:556-573); arrival never clears nav — there is no `NavState::Idle` write anywhere in the step path (all nav write sites: ingest.rs:119,130; economy.rs:810,826; world.rs:427 reset, world.rs:859 dv-decrement rewrite; pirate.rs:484); food resets to exactly grubstake at starvation (pirate.rs:679-681); hungry is strict `<` (pirate.rs:608-610); encounters are pure geometry + lie-low/cooldown gates (pirate.rs:131-165). The post-refuge pirate inherits the haven as lurk and hunts where it fences. D4-4(a) is the right call.
- The marooned breakout IS uniform-over-all-huntable in code (pirate.rs:466-476) — D4 describes the code correctly. Note the fn's own doc header (pirate.rs:443-444) still says "none in reach → the NEAREST station": a stale doc that has already misled one grounding pass (G1 #17). Fix the doc in the same commit.
- Band block (scenario.rs:216-227 + `..TrophicCfg::default()`) confirmed: `pirate_max_reach_au` is NOT set (default 0.6, config.rs:303 region) — D4 §1's "set it explicitly" is correct and config-hash-neutral on the old band (identical value ⇒ identical fold).
- Saturated-guard arithmetic is INTEGER division (scenario.rs:378-385): 6×8333/12333 = 4 ≤ 4 today; at pirates=n=10, 10×8333/12333 = 6 ≤ 8. D4-2(b)'s numbers hold exactly.
- Food-band identities at 15k: 5×15,000 = 75,000 ≥ 2×12×2000 = 48,000 ✓; grubstake/ransom clauses unchanged ✓ (test forms at scenario.rs:392-404). The ×1.56 dilution estimate is consistent with banked data: `runs/media_baseline/*.out` print `laden_trips_per_window` mean 28.36 / 27.96 → ~4.7 arrivals/station/window ≈ D4's "~5".
- Haven-vendor settle pin exists in the shape test ("the hideout station is a vendor (the pirate-haven settle path)", scenario.rs:~357-361); pirate escort intent gated on lying-low + at-hideout + full price (economy.rs:1041-1067) ✓.
- Reset scatter excludes the haven (world.rs:407-419) ✓; `seek_body` re-derives dv from current fuel (pirate.rs:481-486) ✓ (D4-3 composition claim holds); per-craft `spec.clone()` per push (scenario.rs:109-130) ✓ (D4-3(b) is pure factory config — but note the current shape test asserts ALL craft share v_e 20.0, scenario.rs:369-373; the new factory's test must intentionally diverge).
- `N_HAULERS = 12` hardcode in `python/analysis/sweep_trophic.py:72`, consumed in the VoI wallet slice (`per_craft_credits[:N_HAULERS]`, :288) ✓; RESULT/MEDIA abort ✓ (:111-116 region); hungry-roamer control recipe matches the fit-provenance doc verbatim (diagnostics.rs:34-38) ✓.
- HHI_NORM_MIN_MILLI=2204 and HOT_PERSISTENCE_SLACK_CHANGES=3 are margin-midpoint fits on 6-station labeled runs with provenance in doc comments (diagnostics.rs:30-57) ✓; CommonKnowledge requires escape ≥950‰ AND coverage-complete (diagnostics.rs:380, 423-429) ✓; `Saturated` has no classify arm (diagnostics.rs:152-155) ✓.
- Determinism/conservation review of the proposals: clean. The §0 fix adds exactly one Piracy draw on the post-refuge tick (then nav holds the new lurk; draw count is a pure function of hashed state); no new RNG streams, no stage moves, no new money/resource legs; food 15k is a mint-rate change in the deliberately non-conserved metabolism ledger (pirate.rs:252-256); all proposed instrumentation is unhashed pure-read. No frame violations found: windows are recorded-never-gated, no taste scalars, no pirate information reads, OD-2 bundling correctly surfaced as a panel decision rather than absorbed.

## CRITICAL findings

**C1 — Self-averaging rebuild: the "frontier-core gradient" mechanism as described mostly does not exist; post-refuge re-entry is a global-uniform mixing operator.** D4 §2/§4 claim "pirates re-enter the world from the frontier — post-refuge draws are reach-bounded from the hideout so hideout-adjacent stations get first-lurk priority." Recompute the geometry: post-§0-fix, the draw anchors at the hideout (pirate.rs:587-597). On the reference map (hideout 3.0 AU, nearest ring 2.706, reach 0.6), the chord ≤ 0.6 condition gives cosθ ≥ 0.983 → θ ≈ 10.6° → the neighbor is in reach only ~6% of the synodic phase. So ~94% of post-refuge draws fall through to the marooned breakout (pirate.rs:466-476), which is **uniform over ALL huntable stations** — every starvation cycle ends in a uniform re-scatter across the entire map, inner hubs included. (Even on TODAY's band: hideout 1.4 AU, ring 1.19 → in-reach ~14% of phase → ~86% breakouts post-fix.) That is the interdiction-equalizer / contention-game death mode rebuilt at the population level: the relocation kernel approaches uniform, and the ONLY anti-mixing force left is fed-camping selection. D4 §4 honestly registers "equilibrium prediction, not guarantee," but it mis-states the mechanism it is betting on, and its window 6 phrasing ("breakout rate concentrates in the outer region") conflates breakout ORIGINS (outer anchors, true) with breakout DESTINATIONS (uniform, the thing that homogenizes danger). Required fixes: (a) state plainly that re-entry is uniform at reach 0.6 and the gradient rests entirely on differential starvation; (b) pre-register a breakout-share and landing-distribution window; (c) put reach-vs-outer-Δr scaling on the table as the lever the console will likely have to move (D4's own print-reach÷Δr echo is the right instrument for this).

**C2 — False "no core change" claim: the runner-side per-pirate lurk JSONL cannot be built from the example binary.** `World.ships` is `pub(crate)` (world.rs:50-56, explicit doc: "Everything else stays private behind StateView / narrow mutators"), and the public read surface exposes only `craft_pos/craft_vel/craft_fuel/craft_credits/craft_is_idle/pirate_contacts` (world.rs:121-130, 546, 608, 625) — no nav destination, no pirate food, no lie-low state. `examples/trophic_run.rs` compiles against the public API only. D4 §5's "Runner-side per-window per-pirate lurk JSONL … pure read of nav + positions … no core change, no event" is unbuildable as written. Consequence cascades: the lurk dwell-time distributions, breakout-rate-by-region, and hideout-commute fraction — the evidence base for D4-1's watch-item and window 6 — all depend on it. Honest fix is small (extend `TrophicSample`/`sample_window` with a per-pirate snapshot, or add a narrow pub read), but it IS a core diagnostics change and must be named as one.

**C3 — The haven-leak severity is materially understated, and that contaminates the calibration plan D4-5 relies on.** D4 calls the leak a "bounded blemish … ≈1–3k ticks per starvation cycle." The arithmetic says otherwise: the haven station (row 5) is the TIER-2 DESTINATION (dest = 3+tier, scenario.rs contracts; qty 15), so a single haven rob mints 15×10,000 = 150,000 food against a 100,000 grubstake (pirate.rs:252-256) → the pirate goes FED and a fed pirate never relocates (pirate.rs:608-614). With p_rob 700‰, rob_cooldown 600, and heat lie-low served IN PLACE at the hideout (re-emerges with nav still resolving the haven), the haven camp is a **self-reinforcing attractor bounded by haven traffic, not by the starvation cycle** — runway from one rob alone is ~12.5k ticks. Even the rob-free dwell is ~1250 (mean slot wait) + ~2500 (geometric stays at stay_milli 500) ≈ 3.7k, above the stated band. Two consequences D4 does not draw: (1) every banked band run (s7/s23 baselines, the chronicle the owner judged) carries this attractor; (2) the HHI=2204 and slack=3 fits (diagnostics.rs:30-57) were calibrated ON leaky-baseline labeled runs — landing the §0 fix invalidates the 6-STATION calibration too, yet D4-5 schedules re-fitting only "on the new map." The re-fit must cover both maps post-fix, or 6-station readings must be explicitly retired.

## High / medium findings (not critical)

- **H1 — The §0 fix changes the owner-judged game, not just hashes.** Post-fix, ~86% of post-refuge draws on the CURRENT band are breakouts (geometry above) — post-starvation pirates scatter map-wide instead of camping the tier-2 hub. The band the owner called "a great story" included the leak. D4 frames the fix cost as golden hygiene; the real cost is a console band re-judgment, which should be named in D4-4. Also: D4 tests §9.6 says "band scenario goldens re-derived once for the §0 fix" — **no band-trajectory golden exists** (only GOLDEN_ZERO_STATE_HASH/zero-world fixtures, hash.rs:129/1108, and GOLDEN_CONFIG_HASH on the `sample()` fixture, config.rs — none moved by a behavior change). The fix moves zero golden literals; the claim is vacuous overcaution.
- **H2 — "Breakout = chronicle-visible frontier signature event" is currently false.** No relocation/breakout EventKind exists (contract.rs:45-117: PirateLieLow is the only pirate-lifecycle event; `seek_body` is a silent nav write). The chronicle narrates events; a breakout is invisible to it. Either an unhashed single-emit event lands (legitimate, cheap) or the claim rests entirely on the C2-blocked JSONL. The watchable is presently unbacked either way.
- **M1 — Synodic arithmetic error (~2.5×), conclusion robust.** "Synodic period of the two outermost rings ~560k ticks": at n=10 linear spacing the outermost pair is 2.706/3.0 AU → T = 205,640/240,071 ticks → synodic = T₁T₂/(T₂−T₁) ≈ **1.43M ticks**, not 560k (a 2.0/3.0 pair gives 287k; nothing plausible gives 560k). The qualitative claim (outer adjacency frozen per seed, ≫ any run) is robust and actually strengthened.
- **M2 — "Spawn rule requires haulers ≡ 0 mod stations" overstates.** `co_orbit(1 + (k % STATION_ORBIT_AU.len()))` (scenario.rs:107-108) accepts any count; the multiple is needed only for an EVEN 2-per-station spread. Fine as a factory invariant, wrong as a code requirement.
- **M3 — Feed model counts only arrivals; loaded departures are also robbable.** A freshly loaded hauler sits at the source laden at rel-speed ~0 for ~16 ticks before the 2e-3 speed gate closes (a·dt = 1.25e-4/tick), so source stations are in the predation field too. Doesn't change the ×1.5 compensation materially, but the "arrivals/station" derivation in §3 and D4-2(b) should say "dock exposures."
- **M4 — D4-3(b) per-role endurance: legal, but name the asymmetry it creates.** Structurally clean (per-craft spec), no taste scalar. But it means the "survive half of the forager model" applies to prey only — predators starve on food, never on propellant. Defensible this rung; the panel should adopt it as an explicit owner-visible asymmetry, not config trivia. (D4 does surface the trigger for unification — good.)

## Minor

- The relocate_lurk_target doc header (pirate.rs:441-445, "nearest station" fallback) contradicts the code's uniform breakout — fix alongside §0; it has already produced one wrong downstream analysis.
- Cite nits: world.rs:1051-1058 is the `prev_inside_dest` copy-forward — it evidences "nav persists" only indirectly; the strong form is the absence of any `NavState::Idle` write in the step path (verified by grep, listed above). All other spot-checked cites (scenario.rs:156, economy.rs:1041-1067, pirate.rs:537-544/556-573/575-583/608-628/638-641/679-681, config.rs:264-266/303-305, diagnostics.rs:30-57/152-155/380/423-429, sweep_trophic.py:72/288, stores.rs:221-224, trophic_run.rs:58/357-361) are accurate.

## Decision-point dispositions under this review

- D4-4 (fix the leak): YES, but rewrite the cost line per H1/C3 — band re-judgment + dual-map re-fit, zero golden churn.
- D4-1 (single hideout): the "mixing chamber" argument is now LITERAL (C1) — re-entry is uniform mixing. The recommendation may still be right, but the rationale must change from "frontier priority" to "uniform re-scatter + starvation selection," and the breakout-share window must be pre-registered before the panel can honestly prefer (a).
- D4-2 (pool scaling): arithmetic verified; sound.
- D4-5 (re-fit): scope must expand per C3.
- D4-6 (100k runs): coupling claims verified (ephemeris clamp/RAM arithmetic checks out at 48 B/sample).
- Panel flag on OD-2 bundling: correctly handled; D4 forces no format bump (verified — no new hashed columns proposed).
criticals: C1 SELF-AVERAGING REBUILD: post-refuge pirate re-entry is ~94% marooned-breakout (uniform over ALL stations) at reach 0.6 from a 3.0 AU hideout (chord<=0.6 only ~6% of synodic phase; ~86% even on today's band) — the relocation kernel approaches uniform, the claimed 'frontier-flux gradient / hideout-adjacent first-lurk priority' (D4 SS2/SS4) mostly does not exist, and the only anti-mixing force left is fed-camping selection; window 6 also conflates breakout origins (outer) with destinations (uniform). Must be restated honestly + breakout-share/landing-distribution window pre-registered + reach-vs-outer-gap scaling named as the likely console lever. Code: pirate.rs:466-476 (uniform breakout), pirate.rs:587-597 (hideout-anchored draw).

C2 FABRICATED CODE CLAIM: D4 SS5's 'runner-side per-pirate lurk JSONL — pure read of nav + positions, no core change' is unbuildable — World.ships is pub(crate) (world.rs:50-56) and the public surface (craft_pos/vel/fuel/credits/is_idle/pirate_contacts, world.rs:121-130,546,608,625) exposes no nav destination, pirate food, or lie-low state to examples/trophic_run.rs. All dwell/breakout/commute evidence for D4-1 and window 6 depends on it; the honest fix is a named core diagnostics change (extend TrophicSample/sample_window or add a narrow pub read).

C3 MEASUREMENT-INTEGRITY: the haven-lurk leak is a self-reinforcing attractor, not the claimed 'bounded blemish, 1-3k ticks' — the haven is the TIER-2 destination (qty 15), one rob mints 150k food vs 100k grubstake (pirate.rs:252-256) making the pirate FED (never relocates, pirate.rs:608-614), with heat lie-low served in place and nav still resolving the haven on re-emergence; dwell is bounded by haven traffic (~12.5k-tick runway per rob), and even rob-free dwell is ~3.7k. Consequence D4 misses: the HHI=2204 and slack=3 calibrations (diagnostics.rs:30-57) were fitted on leaky-baseline labeled runs, so the SS0 fix invalidates the 6-STATION calibration too — D4-5's re-fit-only-on-the-new-map scope is wrong; re-fit both maps post-fix or explicitly retire old-map readings. Also (H1) the fix changes the owner-judged band's felt game (~86% post-refuge breakouts on today's map): a console re-judgment, not just hash hygiene — and contra tests SS9.6, no band-trajectory golden exists to re-derive.
