# D5 — Lab + Scope Steward: instruments, ensembles, recalibration, landing plan

All paths relative to `/home/john/jumpgate`. HEAD = 88a5d85 (verified). Every numeric claim below was read from code at this HEAD or measured in this session.

## 0. The one new fact I measured

A full 50k-tick `trophic_run` release build executes in **0.87 s wall** (measured this session: `RESULT seed=7 ... robs=39 laden_trips=709 purchases=49`, `real 0m0.872s`). A 100k-tick run is ~2 s. Therefore the entire proposed standard grid (20 seeds × 6 arms × 100k ticks ≈ 120 runs) costs **~4 CPU-minutes single-threaded, under 30 s parallelized**. The basin-chaos lesson ("4 seeds was far too few") has no compute excuse: big ensembles are the default because they are free. Every "is the ensemble too expensive" argument is dead on arrival.

## 1. Ensemble defaults (the standard lab grid, post-basin-chaos)

**The rule: no recorded reading (RESULT/MEDIA/FUEL line entering the ledger, a memory file, or an owner conversation) may come from fewer than 20 seeds.** The 3-seed default in `sweep_trophic.py:303` (`default=[7, 11, 13]`) is a dev-loop smoke setting that has repeatedly leaked into conclusions; it gets demoted, not deleted.

- **Seeds:** 0–19 (the basin-clean precedent, the exact set that exposed the +9.4M retention bleed). Change `sweep_trophic.py` default to `range(20)`; keep `--seeds 7 11 13` available, but the sweep header must print `SMOKE (n<20): not a recordable reading` when seed count < 20 — a printed window, not a refusal.
- **Arms (the standard grid):** {media-off / gossip hear-anchored / gossip rob-anchored (`media.staleness_from_rob_tick=true`, scenario.rs apply_knob arm verified live in grounding G4.10)} × {scenario_trophic, scenario_frontier}. Six arms. The trophic arms are the *anchor*: every frontier reading is reported as a delta against the same-seed trophic arm, never as an absolute.
- **Run length:** trophic arms stay 50k (their calibrations are fitted there); **frontier standard run = 100k ticks** (see §3 for the derivation). `WINDOW_TICKS` stays 2000 (`diagnostics.rs:24`) — do not move two calibration anchors in one rung.
- **Positive control:** the hungry-roamer instrument-kill (`sweep_trophic.py:310-316, 356-360`: `reach=999, stay=0, upkeep=200, grubstake=2e9` must read RiskEqualized on ALL runs) is re-run **on the frontier map** before any frontier reading is recorded. reach=999 trivially covers a 3 AU map; if the control fails there, the instrument broke under map growth and gets fixed before any tuning.
- **The N_HAULERS trap is a phase-0 fix.** `sweep_trophic.py:72` hardcodes `N_HAULERS = 12` and slices the VoI wallet pool with it (`:288`). The frontier scenario will have a different hauler count (haulers must be a multiple of station count, scenario.rs:108). Fix: the runner emits `n_haulers=` in its header (or RESULT line) and the sweep parses it — line and regex in the same commit (the lockstep rule, G4.17). This lands BEFORE any frontier run exists, so the trap can never fire.

## 2. Diagnostics recalibration when n_stations grows

Three constants were **fitted on 6-station labeled runs** and do not transfer; one classifier clause **hardens mechanically** with n. Each is a lying-instrument risk if untouched.

1. **`HHI_NORM_MIN_MILLI = 2204`** (`diagnostics.rs:44`). Its own doc (diagnostics.rs:30-44) says the equalized floor reads ≈ 1000·k/m and SHRINKS as occupied-route count m grows; the 2204 midpoint sits between clumped 2918–3498 and equalized 1472–1490 measured at 6 stations (d41ed82). **The d41ed82 labeled-run method must be re-run at the new n**: hungry-roamer recipe generates labeled-equalized runs, default frontier band generates labeled-clumped runs, fit the new margin midpoint. Do NOT substitute an analytic n-aware normalization without the labeled fit — the analytic form is a hypothesis the labeled runs test, not a replacement for them (see decision D5 for the constant-vs-formula choice).
2. **`HOT_PERSISTENCE_SLACK_CHANGES = 3`** (`diagnostics.rs:57`), fitted at 1–3 robs/window. The frontier map is SPARSER per window (longer legs, G1.23), so the rob-argmax is noisier and the slack likely widens. Same labeled re-fit, same commit.
3. **CommonKnowledge coverage clause** (`diagnostics.rs:380, 423-429`: escape ≥ 950‰ AND `stations_with_news == n_stations`): more stations mechanically hardens the AND-clause, so part of the *expected* saturation→Localized shift is built into the classifier's definition, not the world. **The lab read must disambiguate**: the frontier MEDIA panel reports the reading AND escaped_milli quartiles AND the per-station news-desert map side by side. Pre-registration sharpened accordingly: the "Localized at the big map" expectation only counts as the genuine phenomenon if **escaped_milli itself falls below ~950**, not merely if coverage fails to complete. (Window, never gate — but an honest window.)
4. **`Verdict::Saturated` arm** (`diagnostics.rs:152-155`, no classification arm; `classify` never returns it): NOT this rung by default. The Saturated band-guard RHS (expected-active ≤ stations − 2, scenario.rs:376-385) *loosens* as n grows, so the regime gets less likely, not more. Named trigger to revisit: frontier baseline ensemble shows ≥ half of windows with all pirates simultaneously active. If triggered, the arm lands with a labeled synthetic per the seed-7 rule (diagnostics.rs:896-899) — never fitted on the run that motivated it.
5. **Per-route width n²** (`diagnostics.rs:439-443`, `route_of` diagnostics.rs:611-622): automatic, no code change; note JSONL and hashed-ring word count grow quadratically (12 stations = 144 routes ⇒ ~1.3k u64 folds/tick in word 29; at 0.87 s / 50k ticks today there is an order of magnitude of headroom).

## 3. Run-length / window resolution of the trip-time tension

Derivation: worst frontier leg ≈ 710 ticks at 2.65 AU under the sqrt braking law (G1.11), vs 445 today. At 50k ticks the per-window event counts fall below every fitted sparsity range (HOT slack at 1–3 robs/window, CYCLE_MIN_ALTERNATIONS=3 needs ≥ 3 boom/bust alternations across 25 windows, diagnostics.rs:28). The three options: grow W (halves window count → cycle detection starves), keep everything (sparser windows → all per-window calibrations drift silently), grow the run.

**Design: frontier standard run = 100_000 ticks, W stays 2000 ⇒ 50 windows** (double today's 25). Per-window event density falls by ~×0.6 (longer legs) but window COUNT doubles, so per-run statistics net-improve; per-window calibrations stay closest to their fitted range; the cycle detector gets more alternation room, not less.

Hard consequence (G1.21): the frontier factory must bake `ephemeris_window = 120_000` (run + 20% margin — bodies silently FREEZE past the window, ephemeris.rs:106-111 clamp). RAM ≈ 13 bodies × 120_001 × 48 B ≈ 75 MB: fine. The trophic band keeps 100_000/50k untouched. dt does NOT move: the brakability guard (world.rs:189-202) caps dt at ~0.316 (1.26× headroom) — a closed lever, on the YAGNI list.

## 4. Fuel instruments (windows, never gates)

**Day-zero principle: the instrument lands BEFORE the field changes**, so the tank re-bake number is *derived from measurement*, not from the spec-§6 80k arithmetic. The raw signal already exists: `ThrustApplied` dv events fire per thrusting tick (world.rs:856-869; G2.20) — no new world state needed for the first instrument.

### 4a. The FUEL anchored line (phase 0, hash-neutral)
New stdout line from `trophic_run`, lockstep regex in `sweep_trophic.py` same commit (the sweep already ABORTS on missing anchored lines, sweep_trophic.py:111-116 — extend that to FUEL):

`FUEL seed= duty_milli= burn_total_milli= min_tank_permille= legs= median_leg_burn_permille= refuels= refuel_spend_micros= strandings=`

- `duty_milli` = thrusting-ticks/elapsed-ticks (from ThrustApplied counts) — the measured duty cycle that replaces the "≤ 80k thrusting ticks" upper bound with a real number.
- `burn_total_milli`, `median_leg_burn_permille` = tank-fraction spent total / per leg (leg = dispatch→Arrival, joinable from existing events).
- `min_tank_permille` = the run's low-water mark of fuel_mass/capacity across craft — the range-pressure headline.
- `refuels`, `refuel_spend_micros`, `strandings` print 0 until the mechanic exists — these are NOT dead knobs (the prohibition is on unbuilt *mechanics'* config); they are columns of an instrument whose first consumer is the phase-0 baseline ensemble, and the FuelEmpty count already lives in RESULT (`trophic_run.rs:384-392`). If the panel prefers, ship the line with only the measured fields and append the refuel fields in the mechanic commit — either way line+regex stay lockstep.

### 4b. The calibration chain for the tank re-bake (the honest derivation)
1. Phase-0 baseline: 20-seed trophic ensemble with the FUEL line → measured duty cycle and per-leg burn at 6 stations (banked).
2. Frontier-with-legacy-tank calibration ensemble: run `scenario_frontier` with the inherited near-infinite tank (1e-9 cap / v_e 20) purely to measure frontier per-leg burn and duty cycle.
3. Derive the frontier tank: capacity = k × (measured worst-leg burn), where k is the owner's stranding-flavor choice (decision D3). The derivation is recorded in the factory's doc comment exactly like the band identities in scenario.rs:376-404.
4. Sweep `k` via a new `craft.fuel_capacity_scale` apply_knob arm (multiplies every CraftInit fuel/capacity; immediate consumer = this calibration sweep, permanent consumer = range-pressure sweeps — a live lab lever, not a dead knob). Note each knob point moves config_hash, as all knobs do.

**The FUEL_EMPTY_EPS trap (G2.6) is a calibration invariant**: the trophic band tank EQUALS `FUEL_EMPTY_EPS = 1e-9` (events.rs:16; scenario.rs:93), so FuelEmpty is structurally unfireable there — the frontier tank must sit well above 1e-9, and the first frontier spec ARMS the edge for the first time in a game scenario. The stranding positive control (4c) exists to prove the armed edge actually fires end-to-end.

### 4c. Stranding positive control (Rust test, not a sweep arm)
A scenario-level labeled fixture (pattern: world.rs:2192's `fuel_mass = 1.06e-9` regression): craft with tank sized to strand mid-leg → assert FuelEmpty edge fires once → `resolve_failures` settles Accepted|CargoLoaded|InTransit (economy.rs:1206-1209) → escrow refunded, cargo sunk into `consumed`, craft Idle and drifting → a refill above eps re-arms the edge (events.rs:49-51) and a second drain fires a SECOND FuelEmpty. This is the instrument-kill for the entire fuel story: if the lab cannot see a stranding in a rigged world, no frontier reading about fuel means anything.

### 4d. NO fuel reading classifier this rung
No new `Verdict` arm (RangeBound or similar). The FUEL line + sweep panels carry the read. Named trigger to revisit: a cross-rung comparison needs a one-word fuel-regime label AND labeled runs exist to fit it (seed-7 rule).

### 4e. New sweep panels (`sweep_trophic.py`, all recorded-not-gated)
- Refuel-spend vs contract-income per hauler (does fuel bite margins; the haves/have-nots wallet read already exists as HHI + dispersion panels).
- Strandings per run across the ensemble vs the pre-registered flavor band (D3).
- Tank low-water-mark distribution (range pressure).
- Fuel stock→price coupling panel IF frontier prices fuel via live `PriceCfg` (today's band pricing is structurally dead — `base_micros: [0;N]`, scenario.rs:242, config.rs:155, G3.12; the priced-refuel scenario needs a live curve, which is the economy lens's design, but the PANEL is mine and lands lockstep with that scenario).
- media_log.py: its MediaCfg-mirror CLI defaults (`media_log.py:199-202`) must receive the frontier overrides or the P(escape) analytic check lies — add the frontier preset in the same commit as the factory.

### 4f. Pre-registered frontier expectation bands (recorded in the runner/sweep text, like media_log.py:71-94's band)
From the owner brief, plus fuel: (1) saturation reading leaves CommonKnowledge toward Localized **with escaped_milli < 950** (the §2.3 disambiguation); (2) median hearing lag > 2500 (relocate_period); (3) hub/backwater news ratio > 3.0; (4) gossip-vs-ring flip share's surviving fraction becomes value-positive on clean seeds (Δ vs ring moves from −0.6M toward ≥ 0); (5) duty cycle and per-leg burn roughly double vs trophic (sqrt law: worst leg 445→710 ticks); (6) strandings inside the D3 flavor band; (7) trophic arms remain bit-identical (digest) and `fuel_empty=0` (the existing `--assert-no-fuel-empty` flag, trophic_run.rs:86, stays on in trophic CI arms).

## 5. Golden-commit inventory (exact hash surfaces, single-cause commits)

The headline: **this rung needs NO HASH_FORMAT_VERSION bump and exactly ONE golden re-pin**, if the panel holds two design lines: (a) refuel intent is a transient column None at every hash point (the `pending_upgrade` precedent — not folded, hash.rs:91-94, debug_assert pattern hash.rs:303-309); (b) `scenario_trophic` is left untouched as the anchor (decision D6), so refuel is bit-identical-inert there (the d092496 precedent: "zero FuelEmpty events ⇒ bit-identical by construction").

| # | Commit (single cause) | Hash surface | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|
| A | sweep N_HAULERS fix + `n_haulers=` emission (lockstep) | none | sweep runs green on banked s7/s23 outputs |
| B | FUEL instrumentation line (ThrustApplied aggregation, runner print, sweep regex) | none — events unhashed, diagnostics unhashed | trophic digest unchanged |
| C | refuel config struct (FuelCfg or TrophicCfg fields) + `craft.fuel_capacity_scale` knob | **GOLDEN_CONFIG_HASH re-pin** (config.rs:745 precedent; fields fold into sample()'s hash by construction) | `print_golden_config` (config.rs:1028) re-derive in-commit |
| D | refuel mechanic: transient `pending_refuel` intent + stage-1d-style settle (wallet→corp transfer, stock[Fuel] debit, `consumed[Fuel]` sink leg, quantized fuel_mass credit on existing hashed word 11, one new event kind) | state-hash format UNCHANGED (v5, hash.rs:123); values move only where refuel fires — nowhere in trophic | **cross-branch digest test** (digest-tests memory): trophic 2000-tick digest identical pre/post; conservation tests §6; zero-state goldens untouched |
| E | `scenario_frontier(seed)` factory + band-identity tests + runner `--scenario` arg (trophic_run.rs:113 currently hardcodes the factory) + sweep passthrough | config_hash differs per scenario by construction, but GOLDEN_CONFIG_HASH is pinned to the `sample()` fixture (config.rs:745, 804), NOT to any factory — **no re-pin** | new factory's own identity tests (§6); existing 108+ tests untouched |
| F | diagnostics re-fit: new HHI/slack constants (or n-keyed form per D5) | none (diagnostics unhashed) | labeled-run evidence cited in commit message, d41ed82 style |
| G | frontier sweep/media_log panels + pre-registered band text | none | lockstep regexes; hungry-roamer control green on frontier |

Explicitly NOT in inventory: the cut-2 format bump (RouteEvidence ring word 29 retirement + risk_appetite ghost, OD-2). Since this rung needs no bump, bundling would manufacture a format change this rung doesn't otherwise require — surfaced as decision D4, not silently absorbed. Note also: frontier's bigger n grows word-29 *content* (ring length is config-cardinality-driven, G1.18) — content, not format; no bump.

## 6. Tests (beyond per-commit verification above)

- **Conservation, both identities, with refuel live**: credit identity Σtreasury+Σcredits+Σescrow every tick (world.rs:3087-3113 pattern) across a forced refuel ladder; resource identity Σstock+in_transit == initial+mined−consumed (world.rs:2626-2647) with the `consumed[Fuel] += units` sink leg — without the sink leg this test fails on the first fill (G3.8), which is exactly the trap it guards.
- **Quantization order test**: integer units decided BEFORE the f64 write — `units = min(affordable, stocked, floor((cap − fuel_mass)/unit_mass))` then `fuel_mass += units as f64 * unit_mass` — i64 debit and f64 credit must agree bit-exactly across replays (replay-check harness already exists, trophic_run.rs `--replay-check`).
- **dv_remaining staleness decision test** (whichever way the world lens decides G2.13): either the dock re-derivation is asserted, or the stale-budget behavior is pinned with a comment — never left implicit.
- **FuelEmpty re-arm + stranding positive control** (§4c).
- **Frontier band-identity tests** mirroring scenario.rs:376-404 at the new n: Saturated guard with the new RHS, food band re-walked against the MEASURED frontier `laden_trips_per_window` (the runner prints THE spec-§4 calibration input, trophic_run.rs:361 — at 6 stations it reads 28.36/window; the frontier number comes from the phase-2 calibration ensemble, then gets pinned in the test), per-tier disjoint destinations at the new wiring, hideout-is-outermost assertion at the new body list.
- **Brakability**: frontier craft spec passes the reset guard (world.rs:189-202) — automatic at reset, but a factory unit test asserts it so the failure is a test name, not a runtime reset error.
- **Labeled-run HHI/slack fit harness** outputs recorded (clumped range, equalized range, midpoint) at the new n.

## 7. Landing-plan order (instrument → mechanic-inert → field → recalibrate → read)

- **Phase 0 — hygiene + instruments + baseline (no behavior change):** commits A, B; then bank the 20-seed × 3-arm **trophic** ensemble WITH the new FUEL instrument (runs/ bank, like runs/media_baseline). Rationale: before/after must be measured by the SAME instrument; an instrument added after the field change can't testify about the before.
- **Phase 1 — mechanic on the old map, provably inert:** commits C, D + stranding control. Trophic stays bit-identical (digest). The mechanic is fully tested before any world it actually fires in exists.
- **Phase 2 — the field change:** commit E. Sub-step inside it: frontier-with-legacy-tank calibration ensemble → measure duty/burn → derive tank via D3's k → bake the tank into the factory spec with the derivation in the doc comment.
- **Phase 3 — recalibrate, then read:** commit F (labeled re-fit), commit G (panels + bands); hungry-roamer control on frontier; THEN the headline 20-seed × 6-arm grid; readings recorded as windows; the owner watches the chronicle for the haves/have-nots arcs. Nothing in phase 3 gates anything — miscalibrated instruments get fixed, expectations get recorded as met/missed, the game gets judged at the console.

## 8. YAGNI enforcement list (named, with triggers where they exist)

Must NOT be built this rung — I will flag any panel design that smuggles one in:
1. Police/navy/bounties; maydays + coverage radius; in-flight detour verb; ship-to-ship relay; pirate gossip reads/couriers; identity/route/time corruption consumers (rob_tick lying stays ARMED-dormant) — all owner-named deferrals.
2. The gym pull (OD-4): console-only until the owner's sequencing call. No DRL arms in any ensemble this rung.
3. Cut-2 format bump (ring retirement + risk_appetite ghost): not this rung unless the owner overrides D4.
4. Rescue/tow/salvage or a free "emergency reserve" for stranded craft — the reserve is shaping by another name; the world's answer is priced refuel + drift-as-chronicle-tragedy (the stranding WINDOW is built; the rescue MECHANIC is not). Trigger: the owner, at the console, finds strandings narratively dead rather than tragic.
5. Fuel reading classifier / Saturated arm (triggers in §2.4, §4d).
6. dt growth (1.26× headroom, closed lever) and any thrust/ARRIVAL_RADIUS retune to reopen it.
7. A scenario DSL / config-file scenario loader — two factories is two functions; resist generalizing at n=2.
8. A new RngStream for refuel — refuel intent must be a deterministic function of wealth/tank/dock state (the `run_purchase_policies` desync-by-construction precedent, economy.rs:997-998); randomness here would be a taste scalar in disguise.
9. Per-craft fuel taste/risk scalars in any form (frame law; the risk_appetite ghost stays a ghost until cut-2).
10. Analytic n-aware HHI normalization WITHOUT the labeled re-fit (the tempting shortcut that skips d41ed82's method).
11. Mid-run station spawn / dynamic map growth — buffers sized once at reset is law (world.rs:377-384, G4.16).
12. Traffic-weighted pirate relocation — the reach knob is the locality lever; `relocate_lurk_target`'s geometry-only type is the firewall (pirate.rs:447-458, OD-6).
design.decisions_md:
# D5 owner decision points

## D1 — Frontier standard run length
The trip-time-vs-statistics tension (longer legs ⇒ sparser windows) must resolve somewhere.
- (a) **100k ticks, WINDOW_TICKS stays 2000 ⇒ 50 windows; frontier ephemeris_window baked at 120k (~75 MB RAM)** — RECOMMENDED. Compute is a non-issue (measured 0.87 s per 50k run); window count doubles while per-window density falls only ~×0.6, so statistics net-improve and no per-window calibration moves twice in one rung.
- (b) Keep 50k: free, but every per-window read sparsifies below its fitted range — the diagnostics quietly leave calibration.
- (c) Grow W to 4000: keeps per-window density but halves window count, starving the cycle detector (CYCLE_MIN_ALTERNATIONS=3) and moving a second calibration anchor simultaneously.

## D2 — Ensemble floor as standing lab law
- (a) **No recorded reading from fewer than 20 seeds; 3-seed runs allowed but stamped SMOKE by the sweep itself** — RECOMMENDED (basin-chaos lesson; cost measured at ~4 CPU-minutes for the full 120-run grid).
- (b) Keep 20 seeds as convention only — history says conventions leak; the 3-seed default has already fed conclusions twice.

## D3 — Stranding flavor (sets the tank-size derivation target k = tanks per worst leg)
This is a game-feel choice, pre-registered as a window, never a gate:
- (a) Zero-by-construction margins (k ≥ ~4): fuel is bookkeeping, refuel a toll booth; safest, dullest.
- (b) **Tail-event tragedy (k ≈ 2–3, targeting strandings in roughly the 1–5% of craft-runs band across the ensemble)** — RECOMMENDED: stranding is rare enough to be a chronicle event ("the ship that didn't make it") and common enough that range pressure shapes play; pairs with drift-forever as the honest in-scope fate.
- (c) Common hazard (k near 1): strandings routine; with no rescue mechanic in scope this likely reads as attrition noise, not tragedy.
The k is derived from MEASURED frontier per-leg burn (phase-2 calibration ensemble), not from spec arithmetic — the owner picks the flavor, the lab derives the number.

## D4 — Is this rung the cut-2 format bump? (the scope brief demands this be explicit)
- (a) **NO — RECOMMENDED.** Refuel is designed transient-column (pending_upgrade precedent): HASH_FORMAT_VERSION stays 5, one GOLDEN_CONFIG_HASH re-pin total, trophic band bit-identical. The RouteEvidence ring retirement + risk_appetite ghost removal (OD-2) keep their named trigger and ride a future rung that genuinely needs a bump.
- (b) YES — bundle the bump now: retires the ring and the ghost a rung earlier, but manufactures a format change this rung doesn't need, breaks the bit-identical trophic anchor mid-map-rung, and couples the map/fuel diff to an unrelated retirement. Only worth it if the owner wants the ghost gone before any new eyes read the code.

## D5 — HHI recalibration strategy at the new n
- (a) **Re-run the d41ed82 labeled-run method at the new n and pin a frontier constant; test the analytic 1000·k/m normalization as a HYPOTHESIS against those labeled runs, adopting an n-keyed form only if it fits both maps' labeled data** — RECOMMENDED. One method, two evidences, possibly one formula.
- (b) Pure analytic n-aware normalization, no labeled re-fit: cheaper, but it's exactly the un-validated shortcut the seed-7 rule exists to forbid.
- (c) Dual hand-fitted constants with no unifying form: honest but leaves every future map a fresh fit; acceptable fallback if (a)'s formula doesn't survive contact.

## D6 — scenario_trophic's permanent role
- (a) **Freeze it as the regression/comparison anchor: tank stays at eps (refuel provably inert there), all band identities and goldens bit-identical forever; scenario_frontier owns the new physics/fuel/price spec** — RECOMMENDED. Every frontier reading reports as a same-seed delta against it, and the whole rung lands with zero trajectory-golden churn.
- (b) Migrate the band itself to the big map (one canonical scenario): conceptually tidier, but re-derives every band identity, breaks all banked baselines, and removes the only same-instrument before/after comparison for the saturation claim — the very measurement this rung exists to make.

## D7 — Saturated classifier arm
- (a) **Defer with the named trigger (≥ half of frontier-baseline windows showing all pirates active), landing only with a labeled synthetic per the seed-7 rule** — RECOMMENDED; the guard arithmetic loosens with n, so the regime gets rarer, not commoner.
- (b) Land it now alongside the recalibration: only defensible if the phase-2 calibration ensemble surprises us by actually producing the regime — in which case the trigger has fired and (a) and (b) converge anyway.

critique.findings_md:
# Adversarial review — D5 (Lab + Scope Steward), lens "lab"

HEAD note: repo HEAD is `db08b51`, one **docs-only** commit on top of the brief's `88a5d85` (`git diff --stat` shows docs/posts/scripts only). All code citations below verified at this HEAD.

## Fabrication audit: PASSES

The headline measurement **reproduces bit-exactly**: I rebuilt release and ran `./target/release/examples/trophic_run --seed 7` → `RESULT seed=7 ticks=50000 verdict=Alive ... fuel_empty=0 robs=39 laden_trips=709 purchases=49`, `real 0m0.869s`. D5 claimed 0.87 s / robs=39 / laden_trips=709 / purchases=49. Not fabricated.

Spot-checked citations, all accurate (some ±5-line drift, facts exact): seeds default `[7, 11, 13]` (`python/analysis/sweep_trophic.py:313`); `N_HAULERS = 12` mirror + VoI slice `[:N_HAULERS]` (`sweep_trophic.py:72`, `:288`); sweep aborts on missing RESULT/MEDIA (`sweep_trophic.py:112-116`); `HHI_NORM_MIN_MILLI = 2204` (`diagnostics.rs:44`), `HOT_PERSISTENCE_SLACK_CHANGES = 3` (`:57`), `CYCLE_MIN_ALTERNATIONS = 3` (`:28`), `WINDOW_TICKS = 2000` (`:24`), `Verdict::Saturated` arm-less (`:155`, `classify` at `:193-203` never returns it); `FUEL_EMPTY_EPS = 1e-9` == band tank `base_fuel_capacity 1.0e-9` (`events.rs:16`, `scenario.rs:93,113,126`) with the `1.06e-9` fixture confirming (`world.rs:2198-2203`); `--assert-no-fuel-empty` at `trophic_run.rs:86`, `--replay-check` at `:85`, factory hardcoded at `:113`, RESULT print `:384-385`, calibration-input print `:361`; `apply_knob` surface has no station/orbit/craft/dt knob (`scenario.rs:260-330`); `GOLDEN_CONFIG_HASH` re-pin comment (`config.rs:745`), `print_golden_config` (`config.rs:1028`); `pending_upgrade` not-folded + debug_assert (`hash.rs:64-70`, `:303-309`); trophic pricing structurally dead (`scenario.rs:242`, `config.rs:155`); ephemeris clamp (`ephemeris.rs:106-111`); `ThrustApplied` per thrusting tick (`world.rs:856-869`); hungry-roamer recipe `pirate_max_reach_au=999,stay_milli=0,upkeep_per_tick=200,grubstake_micros=2000000000` (`sweep_trophic.py:320-321`, restated `:355-361`); media_log MediaCfg-mirror CLI args (`media_log.py:198-202`) and the pre-registered band text (`:71-94`). Saturated band-guard arithmetic confirmed (`scenario.rs:376-385`). Derived numbers recomputed: 13×120,001×48 B ≈ 75 MB ✓; 445/710 ≈ 0.63 density factor ✓; 120-run grid ≈ 4-5 CPU-min ✓.

## CRITICAL 1 — The trophic-inertness claim (commits D/F table, D6a, phase 1) is ASSERTED, never DESIGNED, and the band's dead pricing makes the naive failure mode a FREE refuel

The whole golden-commit inventory rests on "refuel ... values move only where refuel fires — **nowhere in trophic**" and D6a's "refuel **provably inert** there." No gating mechanism is ever named. Check the actual band: every station price is 0 (`scenario.rs:242` `PriceCfg::default()`, `config.rs:155` `base_micros: [0;N]`), so the BuyUpgrade-pattern affordability gate `credits >= price` is **always true at price 0**; stations 0-2 hold live `Resource::Fuel` stock (`scenario.rs:150-157`); haulers dock there every Fuel-return leg. A `pending_upgrade`-pattern refuel verb (the design's own template) therefore FIRES in trophic unless something stops it — mutating hashed word 11 (`hash.rs:36`), pumping `consumed[Fuel]` sink legs into the anchor scenario's resource identity, and breaking the bit-identical anchor plus every banked baseline that D6/phase-0 depend on. The only implicit guard is `floor((cap − fuel_mass)/unit_mass) == 0` at the 1e-9 tank — which is true only for `unit_mass > ~1e-9`, an arithmetic relationship the design never states, owns, or tests. The cited d092496 precedent ("zero FuelEmpty ⇒ bit-identical by construction") does NOT transfer: FuelEmpty is an *event* that structurally cannot fire (`events.rs:50` vs `scenario.rs:93`); refuel intent is *policy-driven* every dock. The cross-branch digest test would catch the break after the fact, but a landing plan whose phase-1 invariant is "inert, mechanism TBD" is not a plan — D5 must name the inertness gate (a structural `sells_fuel` station bit defaulting absent in trophic, an explicit FuelCfg-disabled inert lever like `engage_radius_au <= 0`, or the quantization-zero arithmetic pinned by a trophic-specific test) and put it in the commit-D row.

## CRITICAL 2 — The FUEL anchored line is a self-averaging rebuild inside the lab's own headline instrument

`duty_milli`, `burn_total_milli`, `median_leg_burn_permille`, `min_tank_permille` are all pooled over **all craft** — 12 haulers and 6 pirates with categorically different thrust lives (contract legs vs. lurk-camp-relocate; fed pirates camp at ~zero duty, `pirate.rs` hunger gate). `ThrustApplied` fires per craft per thrusting tick (`world.rs:856-869`), so the pooled scalar is exactly the LLN homogenization this project has now died on twice (contention-game 5th NO-GO; the 6-station gossip saturation — "the LLN death rebuilt one layer up"). The recorded reading — the thing entering ledgers and the tank-derivation chain (§4b step 3 derives capacity from "measured worst-leg burn") — averages the field it exists to differentiate: frontier range pressure is a HAULER-leg phenomenon, diluted by pirate camp-time; pirate endurance on long hops is its own watchable, erased by hauler volume. The fix is one line of design: role-split the FUEL line (`hauler_duty_milli= pirate_duty_milli= hauler_worst_leg_burn= ...`) or emit per-role rows in the JSONL with the anchored line carrying hauler numbers only. The 4e "low-water-mark distribution" panel does not rescue the anchored line, and §4b's calibration chain consumes the line, not the panel.

## HIGH — Phase-2 "frontier-with-legacy-tank" calibration can silently exhaust the tank with NO witness, corrupting the very measurement the tank derivation rests on

The legacy tank is 80,000 *full-throttle* ticks (`ship.rs:47` arithmetic; `scenario.rs:21-22`). The frontier calibration run is **100,000 ticks** (D1a) with ~1.6× more thrust-per-trip (longer legs). If realized duty exceeds 0.8, fuel hits exactly 0.0 — and because tank == `FUEL_EMPTY_EPS`, the edge is **unfireable** (`events.rs:50` needs `prev > 1e-9` strictly): thrust dies silently (`ship.rs:35` `fuel_mass <= 0.0`), the craft coasts forever, its contract stays InTransit with escrow locked for the rest of the run (no FuelEmpty ⇒ `resolve_failures` never triggers, `world.rs:1003-1018`), and the measured duty/per-leg-burn distribution is corrupted precisely in the long-leg tail that sets `k`. D5 calls the legacy tank "near-infinite" without this arithmetic; the margin is within ~2× of the cliff and the failure mode produces no event, no RESULT anomaly (`fuel_empty=0` reads as *clean*). Fix: the calibration arm must inflate the tank (e.g. the design's own `fuel_capacity_scale` knob at ×100) so endurance is provably ≥ run length, and/or the FUEL line's `min_tank_permille` must be checked >0 on every calibration run before the burn numbers are banked.

## HIGH — "Same-seed delta against the trophic arm" is a fake pairing, and it compares 100k-tick readings against 50k-tick readings

Frontier(seed s) and trophic(seed s) share only the `mix(seed, k+1)` inputs; different body lists, axes, station/hauler/pirate counts, and — per D1 — different RUN LENGTHS. There is no matched-pair variance reduction; "reported as a delta ... never as an absolute" (§1) dresses a between-population comparison as a paired one, and run-cumulative RESULT fields (robs, laden_trips, final credits, purchases) are not rate-normalized, so every "delta" silently embeds a 2× exposure difference. The honest read is distribution-vs-distribution across the ensemble with per-window or per-tick normalization (the per-window-normalized HHI panel, `sweep_trophic.py:195-216`, already shows the right pattern). The basin-chaos lesson was about ensemble SIZE; this design re-imports a subtler statistics error at the comparison step.

## MEDIUM-HIGH — The §2 recalibration inventory is incomplete: every half-split classifier re-scopes at 50 windows, and D1's "no calibration moves twice" rhetoric oversells

Doubling run length changes more than HHI/slack/coverage: `predation_collapsed` splits at `samples.len()/2` with `PEACE_MIN_EARLY_ENGAGEMENTS = 4` (`diagnostics.rs:211`, `:217-226`) — an absolute threshold over an early half that doubles from ~12 to 25 windows, making PermanentPeace mechanically easier; `media_classify`'s StaleEcho clause splits the same way (`diagnostics.rs:413-421`); the trophic-vs-frontier verdict comparison thus runs two different effective classifiers. D1a's claim "no per-window calibration moves twice in one rung" is true only of `WINDOW_TICKS`; run-length itself IS a calibration anchor for the half-split family and §2 must list it (re-walk or explicitly record the semantic shift).

## MEDIUM — The frontier hungry-roamer "instrument-kill" is circular as sequenced

Phase 3 order is: commit F fits the new HHI/slack constants on labeled runs **generated by the hungry-roamer recipe** (§2.1), THEN the hungry-roamer control runs and "must read RiskEqualized." Post-fit, the control validates the fit against its own training population — it passes by construction. To keep its kill-power: run the control on frontier BEFORE the re-fit with the old constant (recording the drift, which is itself the §2.1 evidence), and fit on held-out seeds from the seeds used to verify.

## MEDIUM — `refuels= refuel_spend_micros= strandings=` zero-columns in phase 0: commit to the alternative D5 itself offers

Printing mechanic columns before the mechanic exists makes `refuels=0` ambiguous between "not built" and "built, never fired" — a small lying-instrument window in the banked phase-0 baseline, and frame-adjacent ("deferred means NOT BUILT"). D5 hedges ("if the panel prefers..."). Resolve it: phase-0 FUEL line carries measured fields only; refuel fields append in commit D (lockstep rule already covers the regex change).

## LOW findings

1. **Sweep ABORT extension vs. pre-FUEL banked outputs**: extending the abort (`sweep_trophic.py:112-116` pattern) to the FUEL line makes the new sweep unable to parse `runs/media_baseline` era outputs; commit A's "green on banked s7/s23" verification is then invalidated by commit B. Version-gate the abort or state that banked re-reads use the banked sweep.
2. **§2.4's "Saturated regime gets less likely" is hypothesis dressed as arithmetic**: the guard RHS (`stations − 2`, `scenario.rs:383`) grows, but the LHS scales with frontier pirate count (undecided) and the food-band re-walk can move `upkeep`/duty. The D7a deferral trigger is fine; the justification overreaches.
3. **`fuel_capacity_scale` scales pirate tanks too** — range-pressure sweeps confound hauler range with pirate hop endurance; also it is the first apply_knob arm mutating structural `CraftInit` Vecs rather than a Cfg scalar (`scenario.rs:260-330` today is Cfg-scalar-only) — fine, but name the precedent.
4. **Commit D never names the refuel settle's stage slot.** "Stage-1d-style" is not an ordering; stage order is law and the digest/conservation tests pin to it. The G2.12 pre-physics precedent (settle before burn, `world.rs:768-786` slot) should be in the inventory row even if the world lens owns the mechanic.
5. **D2's "no recorded reading may come from fewer than 20 seeds"** is gate-flavored vocabulary, but it governs lab evidence hygiene, not the game, and the SMOKE stamp keeps it a window — acceptable, correctly surfaced as an owner decision.

## What survives the attack (credit where due)

The compute measurement is real and reproduced; the golden-commit inventory's hash reasoning is correct at every point I checked (transient-column ⇒ no format bump per `hash.rs:64-70`; GOLDEN_CONFIG_HASH pinned to `sample()` not the factory per `config.rs:804`; word-29 growth is content not format); the N_HAULERS trap fix, the FUEL_EMPTY_EPS == tank trap surfacing (independently confirmed: the band tank sits exactly AT the eps, `scenario.rs:93` vs `events.rs:16`), the instrument-before-field phase ordering, the §6 quantization-order test, the explicit D4 cut-2 non-bundling, and the 12-item YAGNI list are all sound and well-grounded. The two CRITICALs are fixable with bounded design additions, not restructures.
criticals: CRITICAL 1 — Trophic-inertness is asserted, not designed: the inventory's 'refuel fires nowhere in trophic' / D6a 'provably inert' has NO named gating mechanism. In scenario_trophic prices are 0 (scenario.rs:242, config.rs:155) so the BuyUpgrade-pattern gate `credits >= price` always passes, stations 0-2 hold Fuel stock (scenario.rs:150-157), and haulers dock there — a naive refuel settle fires FREE in the anchor scenario, mutating hashed word 11 and pumping consumed[Fuel] legs, breaking the bit-identical anchor and all banked baselines. The implicit guard (quantized units == 0 at the 1e-9 tank) is unstated and untested; the d092496 'bit-identical by construction' precedent does not transfer from an unfireable event to a policy-driven intent. Design must name the inertness gate (structural sells_fuel bit / FuelCfg inert lever / pinned quantization-zero test).

CRITICAL 2 — Self-averaging rebuild inside the headline instrument: the FUEL anchored line pools duty_milli / median_leg_burn / min_tank over all craft, averaging 12 haulers with 6 pirates whose thrust lives are categorically different (hunger-gated camping vs contract legs). The recorded reading — which the §4b tank derivation consumes — homogenizes the exact field (hauler range pressure, pirate hop endurance) the rung exists to differentiate; this is the contention-game/gossip-saturation LLN death rebuilt one layer up, in the lab itself. Fix: role-split the FUEL line (or anchored line = hauler-only with per-role JSONL rows).

synthesis.consensus_md:
# Consensus — "THE WORLD GETS BIG" (map + refuel + instruments, one rung)

All five lenses and all five adversarial critiques converge on the following. Every item below was verified in code this session at HEAD db08b51 (docs-only commit atop 88a5d85; `git diff --stat 88a5d85..HEAD -- crates/ python/` empty).

## C-1. The rung is genuinely ONE rung, not two
Fuel cannot bind from map growth alone: the band tank `base_fuel_capacity: 1.0e-9` (scenario.rs:93) EQUALS `FUEL_EMPTY_EPS = 1e-9` (events.rs:16) and the edge needs `fuel_prev > eps` strictly (events.rs:49-51), so FuelEmpty is **structurally unfireable** in the game scenario — not rare, impossible (fuel starts AT eps and is monotone non-increasing; single write site `(fuel - fuel_consumed).max(0.0)`, world.rs:854). Endurance is 80k full-throttle ticks (1e-12/20·0.25 = 1.25e-14/tick, ship.rs:42-48). Map growth, the tank/eps re-bake, and the refuel verb must land together or the rung's motivation (3) is false. All five lenses independently surfaced this (G1#24, G2#6, D2§0, D3-CRIT-2, D5§4b).

## C-2. Map growth is a NEW FACTORY, never a knob
`apply_knob` has no station/orbit/body/craft/dt knob (scenario.rs:260-330); station wiring is literal-index structural config (scenario.rs:150-207). `scenario_trophic` stays untouched as the permanent 6-station control arm; `scenario_frontier(seed)` is a sibling factory. Zero existing goldens move: `GOLDEN_CONFIG_HASH` is pinned to the `sample()` fixture (config.rs:745,804), not to any factory; zero-state literals (hash.rs:129, 1108) are fixture-pinned.

## C-3. NO HASH_FORMAT_VERSION bump; OD-2 (media-rung: ring retirement + risk_appetite ghost) is NOT this rung
Refuel intent rides a transient column None at every hash point (the `pending_upgrade` precedent: not folded, hash.rs:91-94; debug_assert hash.rs:303-309). RouteEvidence at n=10 grows word-29 *content* (reset-time cardinality, world.rs:365-372), not format. Exactly one `GOLDEN_CONFIG_HASH` re-pin (RefuelCfg fields fold into the sample fixture) plus one new frontier trajectory golden. All five lenses + critiques agree: do not bundle the cut-2 retirements; the ring is the live counterfactual instrument for the gossip-vs-ring window this rung pre-registered.

## C-4. dt is a closed lever
Brakability guard `a_max_empty·dt² < ARRIVAL_RADIUS/(2·k_brake)` (world.rs:189-202) caps dt at ~0.316 (1.26× headroom). Trip time scales as √distance under the braking law (autopilot.rs:83); the worst frontier leg is ~1010 ticks (verified geometry, D1 critique §A). The trip-time/statistics tension resolves by run length, not dt or window size.

## C-5. Run/ensemble lab law
Frontier standard run = 100k ticks; `WINDOW_TICKS` stays 2000 (diagnostics.rs:24) ⇒ 50 windows; frontier `ephemeris_window = 120_000` (bodies silently freeze past the window, ephemeris.rs:106-111; ~75 MB, fine) plus a runner abort on `ticks > ephemeris_window`. Trophic arms stay 50k (calibrations fitted there). Ensemble floor: **no recorded reading from fewer than 20 seeds** (sweep stamps `SMOKE` below that — a printed window, not a refusal). Compute is free: 0.87 s per 50k run, measured and independently reproduced bit-exactly by D5's critique.

## C-6. The fitted instruments do NOT transfer and the trap class is real
`HHI_NORM_MIN_MILLI = 2204` and `HOT_PERSISTENCE_SLACK_CHANGES = 3` were fitted on 6-station labeled runs and are map-size-sensitive by their own docs (diagnostics.rs:30-57); the CommonKnowledge coverage clause hardens mechanically with n (diagnostics.rs:380,423-429) so the saturation read must disambiguate via escaped_milli + per-station desert map. `N_HAULERS = 12` is a hardcoded sweep mirror (sweep_trophic.py:72, consumed at :288) that fires the moment hauler count changes. media_log.py's MediaCfg-mirror CLI defaults (media_log.py:198-202) need frontier overrides in the same commit. Line + regex land in the same commit (the lockstep rule).

## C-7. Self-averaging is the rung's central scientific risk — in the world AND in the lab
Three independent critiques found the same death mode rebuilt: hub-and-spoke wiring with position-blind reward-argmax ASSIGN (verified: no position, time, or fuel term anywhere in the loop, economy.rs:533-645) keeps gossip diameter ~3 hops at any map width (GEO-C2); per-craft "workplace" has no producing mechanism (PLAY-C3); post-refuge pirate re-entry approaches a uniform mixer (TROPHIC-C1); and the pooled FUEL line averages haulers with pirates (LAB-C2). The consensus answer: partition the traffic graph structurally (wiring), role-split the instruments, pre-register the null outcomes honestly, and name the next lever (dispatch locality as a *priced world mechanic*) in the deferred ladder rather than patching scores.

## C-8. Frame compliance (PDR-0006) is clean across the merged cut
Every metric is a recorded window; named triggers route to owner conversations, never gates. No taste scalars anywhere (refuel desync is by construction through wealth and docking history — the documented mechanism, economy.rs:997-998). Pirates stay information-blind at all three layers (world.rs:295-302, media.rs:278-280, pirate.rs:289-290) and `relocate_lurk_target` keeps its geometry-only type (pirate.rs:447-458). Reward stays delta-credits; no shaping; deferred means not built (refuel is gated inert by `lot_mass == 0`, not by a dead knob at a magic value). No new RngStream.

## C-9. The paid-station-service template is settled
Refuel clones BuyUpgrade end-to-end: transient intent column (stores.rs:199-203 shape), policy writer at 1c3 that never clobbers ingest intent (economy.rs:1037-1038), same-tick always-consume-then-gate settle at a dock predicate sampled at the t−1 frame (economy.rs:853-872, 931-952), pure saturating wallet→corp transfer (economy.rs:907-910), §8-totality deterministic skips, single event emit. The ONE new problem the precedent does not solve — the f64 tank vs i64 stock/price quantization — is solved by deciding integer units before any write (G3#17), and the stock debit pairs with a `consumed[Fuel] += units` sink leg (the lost-cargo precedent, economy.rs:1300-1304) so both conservation identities (world.rs:2631-2672, world.rs:3087-3113) hold exactly.

## C-10. The haven-lurk leak is real and must be fixed first
Code-vs-doc mismatch verified: `nav_lurk` resolves any station on the nav body (pirate.rs:578-583) against its own doc ("seeking the hideout → None", pirate.rs:575-577); the hideout body hosts a station (scenario.rs:156); arrival never clears nav (no `NavState::Idle` write in the step path). The critique sharpened it: the haven is the tier-2 destination, one rob mints 150k food vs the 100k grubstake, a fed pirate never relocates (pirate.rs:608-614) — a self-reinforcing attractor, not a blemish, sitting inside every banked baseline AND inside the 6-station HHI calibration. Fix lands phase 0, before anything is banked.
synthesis.recommended_cut_md:
# Recommended cut — scenario_frontier + the propellant loop + the instruments that can see them

One buildable cut. Cross-lens conflicts are resolved by name in §9; every CRITICAL finding is dispositioned by name in §10. Scope = map + refuel + instruments; everything else is in the deferred ladder (§11) with a named trigger.

## §1 The map: 10 stations, geometric band 0.35 → 3.0 AU (from D1, geometry verified by its critique)

`FRONTIER_ORBIT_AU: [f64; 10] = [0.35, 0.4444, 0.5642, 0.7163, 0.9095, 1.1547, 1.4660, 1.8613, 2.3631, 3.0]` — a_k = 0.35·r^k, r = (3.0/0.35)^(1/9), endpoints exact, law pinned by test. Recomputed and confirmed by the adversarial pass: radial gaps 0.094→0.637 AU; against `pirate_max_reach_au = 0.6` (config.rs:303) the 8–9 gap (0.637) NEVER opens — one hop haulers can fly and pirates can never walk. Adjacent-pair open fractions run 54% (inner) → 0% (outermost); outer synodic periods (~1.4M ticks for 8–9, corrected per TROPHIC critique M1) freeze frontier adjacency per seed — the cross-seed haves/have-nots substrate. Star + 10 station bodies (body k+1 hosts station row k), seed-derived phases only via the existing `mix` (scenario.rs:55-65). Anti-memorization unchanged.

Populations: **20 haulers (2/station), 10 pirates** — a 2:1 predator:prey DESIGN CHOICE carried from the band, NOT a guard-derived cap (GEO-C1): the Saturated guard is integer floor division (scenario.rs:379-385) and admits up to 13 at n=10; the guard test is kept as documentation of the ceiling (10·8333/12333 = 6 ≤ 8 with slack).

Physics block verbatim: dt 0.25, softening 1e-4, substeps {3e-4, 64}; brakability arithmetic unchanged (6.25e-5 < 1e-4, world.rs:189-202). `ephemeris_window: 120_000`; runner aborts when `ticks > ephemeris_window` (new guard — nothing checks today, ephemeris.rs:106-111 clamps silently).

## §2 Wiring: partitioned tier loops (the GEO-C2 resolution — wiring, not more AU)

The traffic graph is partitioned by radial band so frontier traffic does not transit the core; this is the named honest fix for the hub-and-spoke mixer, replacing D1's all-tiers-source-at-{0,1,2} wiring (loser: D1 §3).

- **Tier 0 (1.0M, qty 5, core):** sources {0,1} → dest 2; Fuel return 2→3 (sink at 3). Vendor at 2.
- **Tier 1 (2.3M, qty 10, mid):** sources {3,4} → dest 5; Fuel return 5→4 (sink at 4). Vendor at 5.
- **Tier 2 (3.9M, qty 15, frontier):** sources {7,8} → dest 9; Fuel return 9→8 (sink at 8). Vendor at 9. The tier-2 return rides the never-walkable 8–9 gap.
- **Haven = station 6 (1.4660 AU, body 7, `hideout_body_index = 7`):** vendor (the pirate escort settle path REQUIRES a vendor at the hideout dock, economy.rs:1041-1067; carry the shape-test assertion), hosts NO producer and NO contract endpoint.

Factory invariants (tests): per-tier dests/sinks disjoint; every station ∈ sources∪dests∪sinks∪{haven}; haven in no endpoint; **every tier loop touches a vendor** (this restores trophic's heavy-haulers-shop-at-their-delivery-dock mechanism — vendors at rows 3,5 in trophic, scenario.rs:154-156 — fixing GEO-C3b); haulers ≡ 0 mod n (a factory invariant for even spread, not a code requirement — scenario.rs:108 is just `k % len`); seam-haven assertion replaces the old hideout-outermost law (scenario.rs:454-461 is the old factory's §10 law, not physics); per-tier Schmitt-stagger initial stocks carried (scenario.rs:139-143 pattern).

Honest registration (not a claim): position-blind ASSIGN (economy.rs:533-645) still lets any hauler chase any tier, so cross-band deadheads exist; the capacity ladder (qty 10/15 vs base capacity 5, filter-at-choice economy.rs:560-565) gates tiers 1–2 to upgraded haulers and is the *plausible* workplace-stratification mechanism. Window W4 measures whether locality emerges; the registered alternative outcome is "the traffic graph still mixes — the next lever is dispatch locality as a priced world mechanic" (deferred ladder, §11).

Band economics carried as the STARTING WALK, not as "same band": upkeep 12, grubstake 100k, ransom 6M, starve_lie_low 4000, engage 5e-4, **food_per_unit_micros 10_000 → 15_000** (D4's dilution derivation: ~×1.56 fewer dock exposures per station-window; identities still pass — 5×15k = 75k ≥ 2 windows of upkeep 48k, scenario.rs:392-404 forms). `pirate_max_reach_au: 0.6` set EXPLICITLY (today the band inherits it silently); the runner echoes reach÷Δr per ring pair. relocate_period 2500 / stay 500 / rob_cooldown 600 / evidence_window 4000 unchanged — they are the yardsticks the pre-registered expectations measure against (rob_cooldown's "~one trip-time" doc, config.rs:258-259, drifts; documented, sweepable). The comparability claim is withdrawn per GEO-C3: scenario_frontier is **a new world sharing the band's economic constants**, and every cross-map read is distribution-vs-distribution with per-window/per-tick rate normalization (never "same-seed paired delta" — the run lengths differ; D5-HIGH fix).

## §3 The re-bake: eps, exhaust velocity, calibration-first (D2's mechanism, D5's procedure)

PLAY-C2 proved no tank-only value works (2.5–3× round-trip spend at v_e=20 is 20–50× BELOW eps). D2's re-bake is adopted because it preserves every trip-time/brakability number (accel 5e-4 full / 1e-3 empty unchanged):

1. **`FUEL_EMPTY_EPS: 1e-9 → 1e-11`** (events.rs:16), own single-cause commit. The two fixture families straddling the old eps are REDESIGNED (lower starting fuel), not just literal-re-derived (FUEL-critique M5): world.rs:2196-2205, economy.rs:2411-2417. Band runs unaffected (band fuel ends ≥ 3.75e-10 at 50k, never crossing 1e-11) — hash-neutral on the band.
2. **Hauler `exhaust_velocity` (frontier spec only): 20.0 → calibrated, analytic prior 1.0.** At 1.0: burn 2.5e-13/tick, endurance 4,000 thrusting ticks = ~2.5× the worst round trip; tank = 100× eps so the edge is LIVE with ~40 limp ticks (burn cutoff is `<= 0.0`, ship.rs:35). Δv 0.693 ≫ worst-leg need 0.073; cruise envelope unchanged. Caveat owned (FUEL-critique M4): intra-tank mass loss now shifts a_max up to ~2× — the 2.5 margin absorbs it; the calibration measures it rather than assuming.
3. **Calibration chain (instrument before field):** phase-0 role-split FUEL baseline on trophic → frontier calibration ensemble with `craft.fuel_capacity_scale = 100` (new apply_knob arm; first CraftInit-vec knob, precedent named) so endurance provably exceeds run length and the burn tail is uncorrupted (D5-HIGH fix; `min_tank_permille > 0` checked before banking) → derive v_e from measured worst HAULER-leg burn × the owner's k (OD-5) → bake with the derivation in the factory doc comment (the scenario.rs:376-404 idiom).

Pirates keep v_e 20.0 per-craft (OD-6; `CraftInit.spec` is already per-push, scenario.rs:109-130; the existing all-craft-share-v_e shape assert intentionally diverges in the new factory's test).

## §4 The refuel verb: BuyUpgrade clone with three critique-driven fixes

**Config:** `RefuelCfg { lot_mass: f64, corp_index: u32 }` (FUEL-C3 fix: corp_index per the ShipyardCfg precedent, config.rs:320-322, + stale-row deterministic skip). `lot_mass == 0.0` ⇒ both stages early-return (the engage_radius idiom, pirate.rs:128/521/666) — **this is the named trophic-inertness gate (LAB-C1 fix)**: scenario_trophic leaves RefuelCfg default-off, proven by a cross-branch 2000-tick digest test on trophic. Reset error if `lot_mass > 0` while `price_cfg.base_micros[Fuel] == 0` OR any station's seeded `initial_price_micros[Fuel] == 0` (the media half-on idiom, world.rs:207-211).

**Surfaces:** transient `CraftStore.pending_refuel: Vec<Option<()>>` (extend the hash.rs:303-309 all-None assert); ingest verb `Refuel`; events below (§6). Frontier lot_mass 5e-11 (20 lots/tank; ~1 lot core leg, ~3–4 frontier leg).

**Stages (ordering-law amendment, explicit):** 1c3b `run_refuel_policies` after `run_purchase_policies` (world.rs:756) — scripted non-pirate craft, docked at ANY station at the t−1 frame (the generalized economy.rs:931-952 predicate), headroom ≥ 1 lot, wallet ≥ unit price ⇒ intent; top-to-full, threshold-free, no taste scalar. 1d2 `resolve_refuels` after `resolve_purchases` (world.rs:776), pre-physics: always-consume-then-gate; deterministic no-op skips for undocked / stock 0 / wallet short / tank full / **`unit_price < 1`** / **stale corp row** (FUEL-C2 fix — the i64 division panic is closed both by the skip and by factory-seeded initial prices). Same-tick burn draws from the refilled tank; `prev_fuel` untouched (stage-4 copy-forward, world.rs:1049, preserves the Class-3 pinning).

**Quantization (integer decision precedes every write):** `need = floor((cap_eff − fuel)/lot)`; `afford = credits / price` (price ≥ 1 guaranteed by the skip); `units = min(need, stock[Fuel], afford)`; then the four legs: `stock −units` / `consumed[Fuel] +units` (the sink leg both identity tests demand, world.rs:2631-2672) / wallet→**Port corp** treasury pure transfer (no credit sink — G3#7) / `fuel_mass += units as f64 · lot` clamped to cap (one rounding; propellant outside both identities).

**Escrow-lock closures (the two reopened d092496 doors, both shut):**
- FUEL-C1: `resolve_refuels` **re-derives `dv_remaining`** for any refueled craft currently Seeking (a deterministic pure function of hashed state — `tsiolkovsky_dv(eff, dry, new_fuel)`, the same derivation both dispatch sites already use, economy.rs:805-812, 821-828). The same-tick 1c-dispatch-then-1d2-refuel race can no longer trap a full-tank craft coasting at `dv_remaining <= 0` (autopilot.rs:61) with escrow locked. Test: post-settle budget equals the fresh derivation. (Loser: the alternative pre-dispatch stage move — rejected to keep the BuyUpgrade stage symmetry and one settle slot.)
- PLAY-C1: **dispatch eligibility requires `fuel_mass > FUEL_EMPTY_EPS`** — one feasibility filter-at-choice in the ASSIGN loop (the capacity-filter precedent, economy.rs:560-565). A stranded craft stays Idle forever; the ADRIFT detector (role Idle ∧ fuel ≤ eps) now matches the true end state; this is world-truth feasibility (a dead craft cannot fly), not shaping. Plus a liveness window: max non-terminal contract age per run.

**Pricing (joint D1-DP8a/D2-D4a, adopted):** PriceCfg live for Fuel ONLY — `base_micros [0, 5_000]`, `cap [0, 40]`, slope 1800; `cap[Ore] == 0` keeps Ore structurally dead (update_prices skips cap-0 rows, economy.rs:300-332). Curve: full stock 1,000 → dry 10,000 micros/unit; full fill ≈ grubstake ≈ 10% of tier-1 reward; the ransom-clamped wallet (pirate.rs:244-250) arms the robbed→broke→stranded arc at low-stock/high-price stations (FUEL-M6 wording fixed: a literally dry station sells nothing at any price). `initial_price_micros[Fuel]` seeded from the curve at factory build. Revenue → new **Port corp** (treasury 0, Yard precedent) — keeps the Yard-monotone panel clean and gives a propellant-revenue read free. The generator–consumer pair lands in ONE rung (OD-5b two-sided law satisfied: the price's consumer is the refuel settle + the wallet gate). The economic-bite null is pre-registered per FUEL-M3: fuel spend ≈ 1–3% of revenue, so the value of asymmetry rides the stranding tail, not the price arc — if the price gradient reads flat, that is the recorded finding, not a failure.

**Stranded fate:** drift forever, ON THE RECORD — `ContractFailed` event + epilogue tombstone (§6). No rescue, no reserve, no ASSIGN range filter (no demonstrated gap; some stranding is the watchable point). Named trigger: median ≥ ~2/20 haulers lost per run reads as attrition-noise → owner chooses re-pricing vs a future rescue rung.

## §5 Pirates on the big map (D4 with its critique folded in)

- **Haven-lurk leak fix, phase 0:** treat `nav_lurk == haven_station` as None → fresh reach-bounded draw (pirate.rs:575-583 made true to its own doc). Cost stated honestly per TROPHIC-C3/H1: this changes the owner-judged band's felt game (~86% of post-refuge draws become breakouts on today's map) — a console re-judgment is scheduled; NO golden literals move (no band-trajectory golden exists — only zero-state/zero-world/config fixtures); the 6-station HHI/slack calibrations are contaminated by the leak and are re-fitted on BOTH maps post-fix.

Stated as the mechanism; breakout share + landing distribution pre-registered (W6); reach-vs-outer-gap scaling named as the likely console lever. The stale "nearest station" doc header (pirate.rs:441-445) is fixed in the same commit.
- **Pirate fuel = per-class endurance spec** (OD-6 rec): pirates keep v_e 20 (80k thrusting ticks); zero new mechanism; the asymmetry is owner-visible by decision, windowed per-role, with a named unification trigger. (Loser: D2 §5 free haven resupply — deferred; FUEL-M2 showed its "tether = tank/2" was a hope, and one survival pressure per rung.) PLAY-C4 is thereby answered: predators cannot strand this rung.
- Reach 0.6 explicit in both factories; marooned-fallback doc fixed; haven exclusion, hunger gate, reset scatter all n-generic (world.rs:396-431) — zero code change.

Today the failure path is verified silent (economy.rs:1218-1221) — the tragedy's consequences become visible.

Phase 0 ships measured fields only; refuel fields append in the mechanic commit (D5-MEDIUM fix). Zero-refuel sentinel per the MEDIA precedent (PLAY-M6). `fuel_empty=0` flips meaning on frontier ("no stranding this seed" — texture, not check); `--assert-no-fuel-empty` stays on trophic CI arms only.

No format bump (v5 stays, hash.rs:123). One GOLDEN_CONFIG_HASH re-pin (RefuelCfg fields; print_golden in-commit, config.rs:745 precedent). One NEW frontier trajectory golden. Zero existing goldens move.

synthesis.owner_decision_points_md:
# Owner decision points — "THE WORLD GETS BIG" rung

Seven decisions. Everything else in the recommended cut is panel-resolved engineering; these are the calls that shape the game you will watch.

---

**OD-1 — Map shape and populations.**
- (a) **n=10 stations, geometric band 0.35→3.0 AU; 20 haulers, 10 pirates (2:1 carried from the band).** Gradient runs 54%→never-opens with exactly one pirate-unwalkable hop (8–9, gap 0.637 > reach 0.6); worst leg ~1010 ticks; n² costs trivial; Saturated guard passes with slack (floor(10·8333/12333)=6 ≤ 8 — note the guard's integer floor admits up to 13 pirates, so 10 is a predator:prey *choice*, not a derived cap). — **RECOMMENDED.**
- (b) n=8, 0.35→2.2: cheaper, but the frontier is one station deep — the regime contrast this rung exists to produce barely exists.
- (c) n=12, 0.35→3.6: richer, but 24 haulers, a longer food-band walk, and no new structure the lab can't already see at n=10 (the √d law means width buys little).

**OD-2 — Traffic topology: partitioned tier loops vs scaled hub-and-spoke.**
- (a) **Partition by radial band** (tier 0 fully core {0,1,2,3}; tier 1 mid {3,4,5}; tier 2 fully frontier {7,8,9}; every loop touches a vendor at its delivery dock). This is the named fix for the verified self-averaging risk: with all tiers sourcing at the core and a position-blind dispatcher (economy.rs:533-645), gossip diameter stays ~3 hops at ANY width and the rung's motivation #1 likely fails. Partitioning is honest scope: it changes the prey-value landscape, so the food band starts at food_per_unit 15k and gets re-walked at your console against measured `laden_trips_per_window`. — **RECOMMENDED.**
- (b) Scale trophic's hub-and-spoke (D1's original): maximum band familiarity, but three critiques independently predict CommonKnowledge persists and the map rung measures nothing new.
- (c) Hybrid (core sources + frontier tier-2 dest only): splits the difference, muddles both readings.

**OD-3 — Haven: placement, darkness, and the lurk-leak fix (one bundled call — they interact).**
- (a) **Haven at the seam (station 6, 1.466 AU), vendor, NO contract endpoint, AND fix the haven-lurk leak now** (post-refuge pirates currently inherit the haven as their hunting lurk — code contradicts its own doc, pirate.rs:575-583, and the haven being today's tier-2 destination makes one rob mint 150k food vs the 100k grubstake: a self-reinforcing rob-where-you-fence attractor sitting inside every banked baseline). Costs stated honestly: the fix changes the band you judged "a great story" (~86% of post-refuge draws become map-wide breakouts) — a console re-judgment session is scheduled, and the 6-station HHI calibration is re-fitted post-fix. The dark haven also makes the news-coverage clause structurally unsatisfiable, so the lab re-denominates coverage to contract-endpoint stations, scenario-conditionally. — **RECOMMENDED.**
- (b) Haven at the new outermost (3.0 AU): raider-coast flavor, but every re-emergence becomes a cross-map breakout flight and lie-low commutes eat 25–50% of the 4000-tick refuge.
- (c) Keep the leak and watch: zero churn, but the leak scales with the map and contaminates every frontier danger-geography window.

**OD-4 — Propellant economy: unify with traded Fuel + first live price.**
- (a) **Refuel draws station `stock[Fuel]` (the miners→refiners→tanks loop closes) at a live demand-deflation price for Fuel only** (base 5,000, full-stock 1,000 → dry 10,000 micros/unit; Ore stays structurally dead), revenue to a new Port corp. Scarcity gets geography, the robbed→broke→stranded arc is armed, and the death-spiral question becomes a measurement (the I2 `fuel_starve` discriminator decides boom-bust vs collapse — either answer is a finding). Pre-registered null: fuel spend is 1–3% of revenue, so the value of information rides the stranding tail, not the price arc. — **RECOMMENDED.**
- (b) Flat config price, stock-decoupled: a vending machine — no scarcity, no geography, fails the rung's "asymmetry becomes valuable" motivation.
- (c) Both resources priced live: two confounds in one rung.

**OD-5 — Stranding flavor (sets k, the tank-endurance multiple the calibration bakes).**
- (a) Zero-by-construction (k ≥ 4): fuel is bookkeeping; the gauge is scenery.
- (b) **Tail-event tragedy (k ≈ 2.5, targeting ~1–5% of craft-runs):** stranding needs compound misfortune (robbed + dry/unaffordable docks), the chronicle gets "the ship that didn't make it," the fleet visibly thins, drift-forever stays the honest fate — narrated end-to-end via the new ContractFailed event and the ADRIFT epilogue, with the escrow-zombie doors closed (dv re-derived at refuel; dead craft never re-dispatched). k applies to the MEASURED hauler worst-leg burn from the calibration ensemble, not spec arithmetic. — **RECOMMENDED.**
- (c) Common hazard (k ≈ 1): routine strandings with no rescue mechanic reads as attrition noise, not tragedy.

**OD-6 — Pirate fuel this rung.**
- (a) **Pirates keep the ×10 endurance spec (v_e 20, per-craft CraftInit.spec — zero new mechanism, no taste scalar: roles already differ structurally).** Predator survival pressure stays food-only this rung; the asymmetry is this explicit, owner-visible decision, windowed per-role (the FUEL line is role-split precisely so pirate endurance is visible). Named unification trigger: ransom income comfortably covers projected pirate fuel spend (readable today via ThrustApplied per-leg spend). — **RECOMMENDED.**
- (b) Free top-up at the haven: prevents zombies and sketches a tether, but the critique showed the tether is governed by lie-low cycles, not tank size — machinery for a hope.
- (c) Shared scarce spec: stranded pirates are permanently lost predators (no despawn, stores.rs:220-224) — monotone decay into accidental PermanentPeace; kills the boom-bust the rung must keep alive.

**OD-7 — Is this rung the cut-2 format bump? (standing instruction: never silently bundled).**
- (a) **NO.** The entire cut needs no HASH_FORMAT_VERSION change (refuel intent is a transient column on the pending_upgrade precedent; the n² ring growth is content, not format) — exactly one GOLDEN_CONFIG_HASH re-pin and one new frontier trajectory golden. The RouteEvidence ring + risk_appetite ghost retirements keep their named trigger; the ring is still the live counterfactual instrument for this rung's own gossip-vs-ring window — retiring it mid-measurement would blind the comparison we just pre-registered. — **RECOMMENDED.**
- (b) YES, bundle now: retires the ghost a rung early at the cost of manufacturing a format change, breaking the bit-identical trophic anchor mid-rung, and blinding W4.
